text.NRSV: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
text.Rotherham: Originally, was, the Word, and, the Word, was, with God; and, the Word, was, God.

text.NRSV: He was in the beginning with God.
text.Rotherham: The same, was originally, with God.

text.NRSV: All things came into being through him, and without him not one thing came into being. What has come into being
text.Rotherham: All things, through him, came into existence, and, without him, came into existence, not even one thing: that which hath come into existence,

text.NRSV: in him was life, and the life was the light of all people.
text.Rotherham: in him, was, life, and, the life, was, the light of men.—

text.NRSV: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness did not overcome it.
text.Rotherham: And, the light, in the darkness, shineth; and, the darkness, thereof, laid not hold.

text.NRSV: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
text.Rotherham: There arose a man, sent from God, whose name was, John:

text.NRSV: He came as a witness to testify to the light, so that all might believe through him.
text.Rotherham: The same, came, for a witness, That he might bear witness, concerning the light, that, all, might believe, through him.

text.NRSV: He himself was not the light, but he came to testify to the light.
text.Rotherham: He, was not the light,—but, that he might bear witness concerning the light,

text.NRSV: The true light, which enlightens everyone, was coming into the world.
text.Rotherham: It, was—The real light that enlighteneth every man—Coming into the world.

text.NRSV: He was in the world, and the world came into being through him; yet the world did not know him.
text.Rotherham: In the world, he was, and, the world, through him, came into existence, and, the world, knew him not.

text.NRSV: He came to what was his own, and his own people did not accept him.
text.Rotherham: Into his own possessions, he came, and, his own people, received him not home.

text.NRSV: But to all who received him, who believed in his name, he gave power to become children of God,
text.Rotherham: But, as many as did receive him, he gave, unto them, authority, children of God, to become,—unto them who were believing on his name:

text.NRSV: who were born, not of blood or of the will of the flesh or of the will of man, but of God.
text.Rotherham: Who—not of bloods, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but—of God, were born.

text.NRSV: And the Word became flesh and lived among us, and we have seen his glory, the glory as of a father’s only son, full of grace and truth.
text.Rotherham: And, the Word, became, flesh, and pitched his tent among us, and we gazed upon his glory,—a glory, as an Only-begotten from his Father. Full of favour and truth.

text.NRSV: (John testified to him and cried out, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me ranks ahead of me because he was before me.’ ”)
text.Rotherham: (John beareth witness concerning him, and hath cried aloud, saying—the same, was he that said—He who, after me, was coming, before me, hath advanced; because, my Chief, was he.)

text.NRSV: From his fullness we have all received, grace upon grace.
text.Rotherham: Because, out of his fulness, we all, received, even favour over against favour.

text.NRSV: The law indeed was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Because, the law, through Moses, was given, favour and truth, through Jesus Christ, came into existence.

text.NRSV: No one has ever seen God. It is God the only Son, who is close to the Father’s heart, who has made him known.
text.Rotherham: No one, hath seen, God, at any time: An Only Begotten God, The One existing within the bosom of the Father, He, hath interpreted [him] .

text.NRSV: This is the testimony given by John when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the witness of John, when the Jews sent forth unto him, out of Jerusalem, priests and Levites,—that they might question him—Who art, thou?

text.NRSV: He confessed and did not deny it, but confessed, “I am not the Messiah.”
text.Rotherham: and he confessed, and did not deny,—and he confessed—I, am not, the Christ;

text.NRSV: And they asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the prophet?” He answered, “No.”
text.Rotherham: and they questioned him—What then? Art, thou, Elijah? and he saith—I am not; The prophet, art, thou? and he answered—No!

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “Who are you? Let us have an answer for those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
text.Rotherham: So they said unto him—Who, art thou? that, an answer, we may give, unto them who sent us,—What sayest thou, concerning thyself?

text.NRSV: He said, “I am the voice of one crying out in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ ” as the prophet Isaiah said.
text.Rotherham: He said—I, am a Voice, of one crying aloud;—In the desert, make ye straight, the way of the Lord: according as said Isaiah the prophet.

text.NRSV: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
text.Rotherham: And they had been sent forth from among the Pharisees;

text.NRSV: They asked him, “Why then are you baptizing if you are not the Messiah, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”
text.Rotherham: and they questioned him, and said unto him—Why, then, dost thou immerse,—if, thou, art not, the Christ, nor Elijah, nor, the Prophet?

text.NRSV: John answered them, “I baptize with water. Among you stands one whom you do not know,
text.Rotherham: John answered them, saying—I, immerse, in water. In the midst of you, standeth one, whom, ye, know not;—

text.NRSV: the one who is coming after me; I am not worthy to untie the thong of his sandal.”
text.Rotherham: after me, coming:—Of whom, I, am not worthy that I should unloose the thong of the sandal.

text.NRSV: This took place in Bethany across the Jordan where John was baptizing.
text.Rotherham: These things, in Bethany, came to pass, beyond the Jordan, where John was, immersing.

text.NRSV: The next day he saw Jesus coming toward him and declared, “Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, he beholdeth Jesus, coming unto him, and saith—See! the Lamb of God, who taketh away the sin of the world.

text.NRSV: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who ranks ahead of me because he was before me.’
text.Rotherham: This, is he, of whom, I, said—After me, cometh a man, who, before me, hath advanced, because, my Chief, was he.

text.NRSV: I myself did not know him; but I came baptizing with water for this reason, that he might be revealed to Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And, I, knew him not, but, that he might be manifested unto Israel, therefore, came, I, in water, immersing.

text.NRSV: And John testified, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it remained on him.
text.Rotherham: And John bare witness, saying—I have gazed upon the Spirit, descending like a dove, out of heaven,—and it abode upon him.

text.NRSV: I myself did not know him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, ‘He on whom you see the Spirit descend and remain is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
text.Rotherham: And, I, knew him not,—but, he that sent me to immerse in water, he, unto me, said—Upon whomsoever thou shalt see the Spirit descending and abiding upon him, the same, is he that immerseth in Holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: And I myself have seen and have testified that this is the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, have seen, and borne witness—That, this, is, the, Son of God.

text.NRSV: The next day John again was standing with two of his disciples,
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, again, was John standing, and, from among his disciples, two;

text.NRSV: and as he watched Jesus walk by, he exclaimed, “Look, here is the Lamb of God!”
text.Rotherham: and, looking at Jesus walking, he saith—See! the Lamb of God!

text.NRSV: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.
text.Rotherham: and the two disciples hearkened unto him speaking, and they followed Jesus.

text.NRSV: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which translated means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, turning, and looking at them following, saith unto them—What seek ye? And, they, said unto him—Rabbi! which meaneth, when translated, Teacher, Where abidest thou?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Come and see.” They came and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four o’clock in the afternoon.
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—Be coming, and ye shall see. They came, therefore, and saw where he abode, and, with him, they abode that day. It was about the tenth, hour.

text.NRSV: One of the two who heard John speak and followed him was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
text.Rotherham: One of the two that heard from John and followed him, was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.

text.NRSV: He first found his brother Simon and said to him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated Anointed).
text.Rotherham: The same findeth, first, his own brother Simon, and saith unto him—We have found the Messiah! which is, when translated, Anointed.

text.NRSV: He brought Simon to Jesus, who looked at him and said, “You are Simon son of John. You are to be called Cephas” (which is translated Peter).
text.Rotherham: He led him unto Jesus. Jesus, looking at him, said—Thou, art Simon, the son of John,—thou, shalt be called, Cephas; which is to be translated, Peter.

text.NRSV: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Follow me.”
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, he desired to go forth into Galilee. And Jesus findeth Philip, and saith unto him—Be following me!

text.NRSV: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
text.Rotherham: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.

text.NRSV: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, “We have found him about whom Moses in the law and also the prophets wrote, Jesus son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
text.Rotherham: Philip, findeth, Nathanael, and saith unto him—Him, of whom wrote Moses in the law, and the Prophets, have we found.—Jesus, son of Joseph, him from Nazareth!

text.NRSV: Nathanael said to him, “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
text.Rotherham: And Nathanael said unto him—Out of Nazareth, can any good thing come? Philip saith unto him—Come, and see!

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said of him, “Here is truly an Israelite in whom there is no deceit!”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saw Nathanael coming unto him, and saith concerning him—See! Truly, an Israelite, in whom is no guile.

text.NRSV: Nathanael asked him, “Where did you get to know me?” Jesus answered, “I saw you under the fig tree before Philip called you.”
text.Rotherham: Nathanael saith unto him—Whence, dost thou, know, me? Jesus answered, and said unto him—Before Philip called thee,—when thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee.

text.NRSV: Nathanael replied, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!”
text.Rotherham: Nathanael answered him—Rabbi! thou, art, the Son of God: Thou, art, King, of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig-tree, believest thou? A greater thing than these, shalt thou see!

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “Very truly, I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto him—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Ye shall see heaven—when set open, and, the messengers of God, ascending and descending unto the Son of Man.

text.NRSV: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
text.Rotherham: And, on the third day, a marriage, took place, in Cana, of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there,—

text.NRSV: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.
text.Rotherham: and Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, unto the marriage.

text.NRSV: When the wine gave out, the mother of Jesus said to him, “They have no wine.”
text.Rotherham: And, wine falling short, the mother of Jesus saith unto him—Wine, have they none!

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to her, “Woman, what concern is that to you and to me? My hour has not yet come.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto her—What part can I take with thee, O woman? Not yet, hath come, mine hour.

text.NRSV: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
text.Rotherham: His mother saith unto them who are ministering—Whatsoever he may say to you, do!

text.NRSV: Now standing there were six stone water jars for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.
text.Rotherham: Now there were there, six stone water-vessels, placed, according to the purification of the Jews; holding each, two or three measures.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Fill the jars with water.” And they filled them up to the brim.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—Fill the vessels with water. And they filled them up to the brim.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the chief steward.” So they took it.
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Draw out now, and be bearing unto the master of the feast. And they bare.

text.NRSV: When the steward tasted the water that had become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the steward called the bridegroom
text.Rotherham: And, when the master of the feast had tasted the water, now made, wine, and knew not whence it was,—though, they who were ministering, knew, even they who had drawn out the water, the master accosteth the bridegroom,

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and then the inferior wine after the guests have become drunk; but you have kept the good wine until now.”
text.Rotherham: and saith unto him—Every man, first the good wine, setteth out; and, when they had been well-supplied, the inferior:—Thou, hast kept the good wine until, even now.

text.NRSV: Jesus did this, the first of his signs, in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.
text.Rotherham: This, beginning of his signs, did Jesus, in Cana of Galilee, and manifested his glory; and his disciples believed on him.

text.NRSV: After this he went down to Capernaum with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they remained there a few days.
text.Rotherham: After this, he came down into Capernaum,—he, and his mother and brethren, and his disciples; and, there, they abode, not many days.

text.NRSV: The Passover of the Jews was near, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, near, was the passover of the Jews; and, Jesus, went up unto Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: In the temple he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and the money changers seated at their tables.
text.Rotherham: And he found, in the temple, them that were selling oxen and sheep and doves, also the money-changers sitting.

text.NRSV: Making a whip of cords, he drove all of them out of the temple, with the sheep and the cattle. He also poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
text.Rotherham: And, making a scourge out of rushes, all of them, thrust he forth out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and, the money-changers’ small coins, poured he forth, and, the tables, he overturned;

text.NRSV: He told those who were selling the doves, “Take these things out of here! Stop making my Father’s house a marketplace!”
text.Rotherham: and unto them who were selling, the doves, he said—Take these things hence! Be not making, the house of my Father, a house of merchandise.

text.NRSV: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will consume me.”
text.Rotherham: His disciples remembered that it was written—The zeal of thy house, eateth me up.

text.NRSV: The Jews then said to him, “What sign can you show us for doing this?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews therefore answered, and said unto him, What sign, dost thou point out to us, in that, these things, thou doest?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Take down this shrine, and, in three days, will I raise it.

text.NRSV: The Jews then said, “This temple has been under construction for forty-six years, and will you raise it up in three days?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said—In forty and six years, was this shrine built. And, thou, in three days, wilt raise it!

text.NRSV: But he was speaking of the temple of his body.
text.Rotherham: But, he, was speaking concerning the shrine of his body.

text.NRSV: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this; and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had been raised from among the dead, his disciples remembered, that, this, he had been saying; and they believed in the Scripture, and in the word which Jesus had spoken.

text.NRSV: When he was in Jerusalem during the Passover festival, many believed in his name because they saw the signs that he was doing.
text.Rotherham: Now, when he was in Jerusalem, during the passover, during the feast, many, believed on his name, viewing his signs which he was doing.

text.NRSV: But Jesus on his part would not entrust himself to them, because he knew all people
text.Rotherham: Jesus himself, however, was not trusting himself unto them, by reason of his understanding them all,

text.NRSV: and needed no one to testify about anyone; for he himself knew what was in everyone.
text.Rotherham: and because he had, no need, that anyone should bear witness concerning man; for, he, understood what was in man.

text.NRSV: Now there was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a leader of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: There was however, a man from among the Pharisees, Nicodemus, his name,—ruler of the Jews.

text.NRSV: He came to Jesus by night and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God; for no one can do these signs that you do apart from the presence of God.”
text.Rotherham: The same, came unto him, by night, and said unto him—Rabbi! we know that, from God, thou hast come, a teacher; for, no one, can be doing, these signs, which, thou, art doing, except, God, be with him.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “Very truly, I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God without being born from above.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—Verily, verily, I say unto thee: Except one be born from above, he cannot see the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus said to him, “How can anyone be born after having grown old? Can one enter a second time into the mother’s womb and be born?”
text.Rotherham: Nicodemus saith unto him—How, can a man be born, when he is, old? Can he, into the womb of his mother, a second time, enter, and be born?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Very truly, I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God without being born of water and Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Verily, verily, I say unto thee: Except one be born of water and spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: What is born of the flesh is flesh, and what is born of the Spirit is spirit.
text.Rotherham: That which hath been born of the flesh, is, flesh, and, that which hath been born of the spirit, is, spirit.

text.NRSV: Do not be astonished that I said to you, ‘You must be born from above.’
text.Rotherham: Do not marvel, that I said unto thee: Ye must needs be born from above.

text.NRSV: The wind blows where it chooses, and you hear the sound of it, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes. So it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: The spirit, where it pleaseth, doth breathe, and, the sound thereof, thou hearest; but knowest not, whence it cometh and whither it goeth: Thus, is every one who hath been born of the spirit.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus said to him, “How can these things be?”
text.Rotherham: Nicodemus answered, and said unto him—How, can these things, come about?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “Are you a teacher of Israel, and yet you do not understand these things?
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—Art, thou, the teacher of Israel, and, these things, knowest not?

text.NRSV: “Very truly, I tell you, we speak of what we know and testify to what we have seen; yet you do not receive our testimony.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto thee: What we know, we speak, and, of what we have seen, we bear witness, and, our witness, ye receive not.

text.NRSV: If I have told you about earthly things and you do not believe, how can you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
text.Rotherham: If, the earthly things, I told you, and ye believe not, How, if I should tell you the heavenly things, will ye believe?

text.NRSV: No one has ascended into heaven except the one who descended from heaven, the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: And, no one, hath ascended into heaven, save he that, out of heaven, descended,—The Son of Man.

text.NRSV: And just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
text.Rotherham: And, just as, Moses, lifted up the serpent in the desert, so, must, the Son of Man, be lifted up,—

text.NRSV: that whoever believes in him may have eternal life.
text.Rotherham: That, whosoever believeth in him, may have life age-abiding.

text.NRSV: “For God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him may not perish but may have eternal life.
text.Rotherham: For God, so loved, the world, that, his Only Begotten Son, he gave,—that, whosoever believeth on him, might not perish, but have life age-abiding.

text.NRSV: “Indeed, God did not send the Son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him.
text.Rotherham: For God, sent not, his Son into the world, that he might judge the world, but, that the world might be saved through him.

text.NRSV: Those who believe in him are not condemned; but those who do not believe are condemned already, because they have not believed in the name of the only Son of God.
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on him, is not to be judged: he that believeth not, already, hath been judged,—because he hath not believed on the name of the Only Begotten Son of God.

text.NRSV: And this is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and people loved darkness rather than light because their deeds were evil.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the judgment: That, the light, hath come into the world,—and men loved, rather the darkness than the light, for, wicked, were their, works.

text.NRSV: For all who do evil hate the light and do not come to the light, so that their deeds may not be exposed.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever doth practise corrupt things, hateth the light, and cometh not unto the light, lest his works should be reproved;

text.NRSV: But those who do what is true come to the light, so that it may be clearly seen that their deeds have been done in God.”
text.Rotherham: But, he that doeth the truth, cometh unto the light, that his works may be, made manifest, that, in God, have they been wrought.

text.NRSV: After this Jesus and his disciples went into the Judean countryside, and he spent some time there with them and baptized.
text.Rotherham: After these things, came Jesus, and his disciples, into the Judaean land; and, there, was he tarrying with them, and immersing.

text.NRSV: John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim because water was abundant there; and people kept coming and were being baptized
text.Rotherham: And John also was immersing in Aenon, near to him, because, many waters, were there; and they were coming, and being immersed;—

text.NRSV: —John, of course, had not yet been thrown into prison.
text.Rotherham: for, not yet, had John been cast into prison.

text.NRSV: Now a discussion about purification arose between John’s disciples and a Jew.
text.Rotherham: There arose, therefore, a questioning, from among the disciples of John, with a Jew,—concerning purification.

text.NRSV: They came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, the one who was with you across the Jordan, to whom you testified, here he is baptizing, and all are going to him.”
text.Rotherham: And they came unto John, and said unto him—Rabbi! he who was with thee beyond the Jordan, unto whom, thou, hast borne witness, see! the same, is immersing; and, all, are coming unto him.

text.NRSV: John answered, “No one can receive anything except what has been given from heaven.
text.Rotherham: John answered, and said—A man can receive, nothing, except it have been given him out of heaven.

text.NRSV: You yourselves are my witnesses that I said, ‘I am not the Messiah, but I have been sent ahead of him.’
text.Rotherham: Ye yourselves, unto me, bear witness, that, I, said—I, am not the Christ; but—I am sent before, That One.

text.NRSV: He who has the bride is the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom’s voice. For this reason my joy has been fulfilled.
text.Rotherham: He that hath the bride, is, bridegroom; but, the friend of the bridegroom, who standeth by and hearkeneth unto him, greatly, rejoiceth, by reason of the voice of the bridegroom. This, my joy, therefore, is fulfilled.

text.NRSV: He must increase, but I must decrease.”
text.Rotherham: That One, it behoveth to increase,—but, me, to decrease.

text.NRSV: The one who comes from above is above all; the one who is of the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all.
text.Rotherham: He that, from above, doth come, over all, is: he that is of the earth, of the earth, is, and, of the earth, doth speak: he that, out of heaven, doth come, over all, is,

text.NRSV: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, yet no one accepts his testimony.
text.Rotherham: What he hath seen and heard, of, the same, he beareth witness,—and, his witness, no one, receiveth:—

text.NRSV: Whoever accepts his testimony certifies this, that God is true.
text.Rotherham: He that hath received his witness, hath set seal—that, God, is, true.

text.NRSV: He whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for he gives the Spirit without measure.
text.Rotherham: For, he whom God hath sent, the sayings of God, doth speak; for, not by measure, giveth he the Spirit.

text.NRSV: The Father loves the Son and has placed all things in his hands.
text.Rotherham: The Father, loveth the Son, and, all things, hath given into his hand.

text.NRSV: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life; whoever disobeys the Son will not see life, but must endure God’s wrath.
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on the Son, hath life age-abiding: whereas, he that yieldeth not unto the Son, shall not see life,—but, the anger of God, awaiteth him.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard, “Jesus is making and baptizing more disciples than John”
text.Rotherham: When therefore the Lord knew, that the Pharisees had heard—Jesus, more disciples, is making and immersing, than John,—

text.NRSV: —although it was not Jesus himself but his disciples who baptized—
text.Rotherham: although indeed, Jesus himself, was not immersing, but his disciples,

text.NRSV: he left Judea and started back to Galilee.
text.Rotherham: he left Judaea, and departed again into Galilee.

text.NRSV: But he had to pass through Samaria.
text.Rotherham: Now he must needs pass through Samaria.

text.NRSV: So he came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, near the plot of ground that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
text.Rotherham: He cometh, therefore, unto a city of Samaria, called Sychar, near the parcel of ground which Jacob gave unto Joseph his son.

text.NRSV: Jacob’s well was there, and Jesus, tired out by his journey, was sitting by the well. It was about noon.
text.Rotherham: Now Jacob’s fountain was there. Jesus, therefore, having become toil-worn with the journey, was sitting thus, upon the fountain. It was about the sixth hour.

text.NRSV: A Samaritan woman came to draw water, and Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”
text.Rotherham: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water. Jesus saith unto her—Give me to drink!

text.NRSV: (His disciples had gone to the city to buy food.)
text.Rotherham: for, his disciples, had gone away into the city, that, food, they might buy.

text.NRSV: The Samaritan woman said to him, “How is it that you, a Jew, ask a drink of me, a woman of Samaria?” (Jews do not share things in common with Samaritans.)
text.Rotherham: The Samaritan woman, therefore, saith unto him—How dost, thou, being, a Jew, ask to drink, of me, who am, a Samaritan woman? [for, Jews, have no dealings with Samaritans.]

text.NRSV: Jesus answered her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is that is saying to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto her—If thou hadst known the free gift of God, and who it is that is saying unto thee, Give me to drink, thou, wouldst have asked him, and he would have given thee living water.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “Sir, you have no bucket, and the well is deep. Where do you get that living water?
text.Rotherham: She saith unto him—Sir! not even a bucket, hast thou,—and, the well, is, deep:—Whence, then, hast thou the living water?

text.NRSV: Are you greater than our ancestor Jacob, who gave us the well, and with his sons and his flocks drank from it?”
text.Rotherham: Art, thou, greater than, our father Jacob, who gave us the well,—and, himself, out of it drank, and his sons, and his flocks?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will be thirsty again,
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto her—Whosoever drinketh of this water, will thirst, again;

text.NRSV: but those who drink of the water that I will give them will never be thirsty. The water that I will give will become in them a spring of water gushing up to eternal life.”
text.Rotherham: But, whosoever shall drink of the water which, I, will give him, in nowise shall thirst, unto times age-abiding,—but, the water which I will give him, shall become, within him, a fountain of water, springing up unto life age-abiding.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I may never be thirsty or have to keep coming here to draw water.”
text.Rotherham: The woman saith unto him—Sir! give me this water, that I thirst not, neither be coming hither to draw.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come back.”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto her—Go, call thy husband, and come hither!

text.NRSV: The woman answered him, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You are right in saying, ‘I have no husband’;
text.Rotherham: The woman answered, and said [unto him]—I have no husband. Jesus saith unto her—Well, saidst thou, A husband, I have not;

text.NRSV: for you have had five husbands, and the one you have now is not your husband. What you have said is true!”
text.Rotherham: for, five husbands, thou hast had,—and, now, he whom thou hast, is not thy, husband: This true thing, hast thou spoken.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
text.Rotherham: The woman saith unto him—Sir! I perceive that, a prophet, art, thou:—

text.NRSV: Our ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you say that the place where people must worship is in Jerusalem.”
text.Rotherham: Our fathers, in this mountain, worshipped; and, ye, say, that, in Jerusalem, is the place, where, to worship, it behoveth.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe me, the hour is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Believe me, woman! There cometh an hour, when, neither in this mountain, nor yet in Jerusalem, shall ye worship the Father.

text.NRSV: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
text.Rotherham: Ye, worship, that which ye know not; We, worship, that which we know; because, salvation, is, of the Jews.

text.NRSV: But the hour is coming, and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father seeks such as these to worship him.
text.Rotherham: But there cometh an hour, and, now, is,—when, the real worshippers, shall worship the Father in spirit and truth; for, even the Father, is seeking, such as these, as his worshippers.

text.NRSV: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”
text.Rotherham: God is, spirit; and, they that worship him, in spirit and truth, must needs worship.

text.NRSV: The woman said to him, “I know that Messiah is coming” (who is called Christ). “When he comes, he will proclaim all things to us.”
text.Rotherham: The woman saith unto him—I know that, Messiah, cometh, who is called Christ,—Whensoever, he, shall come, he will tell us, everything.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “I am he, the one who is speaking to you.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—I, that speak unto thee, am he.

text.NRSV: Just then his disciples came. They were astonished that he was speaking with a woman, but no one said, “What do you want?” or, “Why are you speaking with her?”
text.Rotherham: And, hereupon, came his disciples, and they began to marvel, that, with a woman, he was talking. No one, however, said—What seekest thou? or—Why talkest thou with her?

text.NRSV: Then the woman left her water jar and went back to the city. She said to the people,
text.Rotherham: The woman, therefore, left her water-vessel, and went away into the city, and saith unto the men—

text.NRSV: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! He cannot be the Messiah, can he?”
text.Rotherham: Come! see a man, that told me all things that ever I did: Can, this, be, the Christ?

text.NRSV: They left the city and were on their way to him.
text.Rotherham: They came forth out of the city, and were coming unto him.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile the disciples were urging him, “Rabbi, eat something.”
text.Rotherham: In the meantime, the disciples were requesting him, saying—Rabbi! eat.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you do not know about.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—I, have, food, to eat, of which, ye, know not.

text.NRSV: So the disciples said to one another, “Surely no one has brought him something to eat?”
text.Rotherham: The disciples, therefore, began to say, one to another—Hath anyone brought him, to eat?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to complete his work.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—My food, is, that I should do the will of him that sent me, and complete, his work.

text.NRSV: Do you not say, ‘Four months more, then comes the harvest’? But I tell you, look around you, and see how the fields are ripe for harvesting.
text.Rotherham: Are, ye, not saying—Yet, a fourmonth, it is, and, the harvest, cometh? Lo! I say unto you—Lift up your eyes, and gaze at the fields,—that, white, are they unto, harvest. Already,

text.NRSV: The reaper is already receiving wages and is gathering fruit for eternal life, so that sower and reaper may rejoice together.
text.Rotherham: he that reapeth, receiveth, a reward, and gathereth fruit unto life age-abiding; that, he that soweth, together may rejoice, with him that reapeth.

text.NRSV: For here the saying holds true, ‘One sows and another reaps.’
text.Rotherham: For, herein, doth the saying, hold good,—One, is the sower, and, another, is the reaper.

text.NRSV: I sent you to reap that for which you did not labor. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.”
text.Rotherham: I, sent you forth, to be reaping that whereon, ye, have not toiled: Others, have toiled, and, ye, into their toil, have entered.

text.NRSV: Many Samaritans from that city believed in him because of the woman’s testimony, “He told me everything I have ever done.”
text.Rotherham: And, out of that city, many, believed on him—of the Samaritans,—by reason of the word of the woman in bearing witness—He told me all things that ever I did.

text.NRSV: So when the Samaritans came to him, they asked him to stay with them; and he stayed there two days.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, the Samaritans came unto him, they went on to request him to abide with them; and he abode there two days.

text.NRSV: And many more believed because of his word.
text.Rotherham: And, many more, believed by reason of his discourse;

text.NRSV: They said to the woman, “It is no longer because of what you said that we believe, for we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this is truly the Savior of the world.”
text.Rotherham: and, unto the woman, began to say—No longer, by reason of thy talk, do we believe; for, we ourselves, have heard, and know that, This One, is, in truth, the Saviour of the world.

text.NRSV: When the two days were over, he went from that place to Galilee
text.Rotherham: But, after the two days, he went forth from thence, into Galilee.

text.NRSV: (for Jesus himself had testified that a prophet has no honor in the prophet’s own country).
text.Rotherham: For, Jesus himself, bare witness, that, a prophet, in his own fatherland, hath not, honour.

text.NRSV: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him, having seen all that he had done in Jerusalem at the festival; for they too had gone to the festival.
text.Rotherham: So, when he came into Galilee, the Galilaeans welcomed him, having seen, all things, whatsoever he had done in Jerusalem during the feast; for, they also, went unto the feast.

text.NRSV: Then he came again to Cana in Galilee where he had changed the water into wine. Now there was a royal official whose son lay ill in Capernaum.
text.Rotherham: So he came again into Cana of Galilee, where he had made the water, wine. And there was a certain courtier, whose son was sick, in Capernaum.

text.NRSV: When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and begged him to come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.
text.Rotherham: The same, hearing that Jesus had come out of Judaea into Galilee, came away unto him, and began requesting him, that he would come down and heal his son; for he was on the point of dying.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “Unless you see signs and wonders you will not believe.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus therefore said unto him—Except, signs and wonders, ye see, in nowise will ye believe.

text.NRSV: The official said to him, “Sir, come down before my little boy dies.”
text.Rotherham: The courtier saith unto him—Sir! come down, ere my child die!

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Go; your son will live.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and started on his way.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—Be going thy way: thy son, liveth! And the man believed in the word which Jesus spake to him, and at once went his way.

text.NRSV: As he was going down, his slaves met him and told him that his child was alive.
text.Rotherham: And, when he was even now going down, his servants, met him, saying, that, his boy, was living.

text.NRSV: So he asked them the hour when he began to recover, and they said to him, “Yesterday at one in the afternoon the fever left him.”
text.Rotherham: So he enquired the hour, from them, when he began, to amend. They said, therefore, unto him—Yesterday, at the seventh hour, the fever left him.

text.NRSV: The father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, “Your son will live.” So he himself believed, along with his whole household.
text.Rotherham: So the father took note that it was, in that hour, wherein Jesus said unto him—Thy son, liveth! and he believed, he, and all his house.

text.NRSV: Now this was the second sign that Jesus did after coming from Judea to Galilee.
text.Rotherham: Now, this again, as, a second sign, Jesus did, after coming out of Judaea into Galilee.

verses.text: After this there was a festival of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
Now in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate there is a pool, called in Hebrew Beth-zatha, which has five porticoes.
In these lay many invalids—blind, lame, and paralyzed.
One man was there who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
When Jesus saw him lying there and knew that he had been there a long time, he said to him, “Do you want to be made well?”
The sick man answered him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up; and while I am making my way, someone else steps down ahead of me.”
Jesus said to him, “Stand up, take your mat and walk.”
At once the man was made well, and he took up his mat and began to walk. Now that day was a sabbath.
So the Jews said to the man who had been cured, “It is the sabbath; it is not lawful for you to carry your mat.”
But he answered them, “The man who made me well said to me, ‘Take up your mat and walk.’ ”
They asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Take it up and walk’?”
Now the man who had been healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had merged into the crowd that was there.
Later Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, “See, you have been made well! Do not sin anymore, so that nothing worse happens to you.”
The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
Therefore the Jews started persecuting Jesus, because he was doing these things on the sabbath.
But Jesus answered them, “My Father is still working, and I also am working.”
For this reason the Jews were seeking all the more to kill him, because he was not only breaking the sabbath, but was also calling God his own Father, thereby making himself equal to God.
Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, the Son can do nothing on his own, but only what he sees the Father doing; for whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
The Father loves the Son and shows him all that he himself is doing; and he will show him greater works than these, so that you will be astonished.
Indeed, just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so also the Son gives life to whomever he wishes.
The Father judges no one but has given all judgment to the Son,
so that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Anyone who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
Very truly, I tell you, anyone who hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life, and does not come under judgment, but has passed from death to life.
“Very truly, I tell you, the hour is coming, and is now here, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
For just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son also to have life in himself;
and he has given him authority to execute judgment, because he is the Son of Man.
Do not be astonished at this; for the hour is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice
and will come out—those who have done good, to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of condemnation.
“I can do nothing on my own. As I hear, I judge; and my judgment is just, because I seek to do not my own will but the will of him who sent me.
“If I testify about myself, my testimony is not true.
There is another who testifies on my behalf, and I know that his testimony to me is true.
You sent messengers to John, and he testified to the truth.
Not that I accept such human testimony, but I say these things so that you may be saved.
He was a burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
But I have a testimony greater than John’s. The works that the Father has given me to complete, the very works that I am doing, testify on my behalf that the Father has sent me.
And the Father who sent me has himself testified on my behalf. You have never heard his voice or seen his form,
and you do not have his word abiding in you, because you do not believe him whom he has sent.
“You search the scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that testify on my behalf.
Yet you refuse to come to me to have life.
I do not accept glory from human beings.
But I know that you do not have the love of God in you.
I have come in my Father’s name, and you do not accept me; if another comes in his own name, you will accept him.
How can you believe when you accept glory from one another and do not seek the glory that comes from the one who alone is God?
Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father; your accuser is Moses, on whom you have set your hope.
If you believed Moses, you would believe me, for he wrote about me.
But if you do not believe what he wrote, how will you believe what I say?”

text.NRSV: After this Jesus went to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, also called the Sea of Tiberias.
text.Rotherham: After these things, Jesus went away across the sea of Galilee, that is, of Tiberias;

text.NRSV: A large crowd kept following him, because they saw the signs that he was doing for the sick.
text.Rotherham: and there was following him a great multitude, because they had been viewing the signs which he did upon such as were sick.

text.NRSV: Jesus went up the mountain and sat down there with his disciples.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus had gone up into the mountain, and, there, was sitting with his disciples.

text.NRSV: Now the Passover, the festival of the Jews, was near.
text.Rotherham: Now the passover was near, the feast of the Jews.

text.NRSV: When he looked up and saw a large crowd coming toward him, Jesus said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread for these people to eat?”
text.Rotherham: So then Jesus, lifting up his eyes, and beholding that a great multitude was coming unto him, saith unto Philip—Whence are we to buy loaves, that these may eat?

text.NRSV: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he was going to do.
text.Rotherham: But, this, he was saying, to test him; for, he himself, knew, what he was about to do.

text.NRSV: Philip answered him, “Six months’ wages would not buy enough bread for each of them to get a little.”
text.Rotherham: Philip answered him—Two hundred denaries-worth of loaves, are not sufficient for them, that, each one, may take, a little.

text.NRSV: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
text.Rotherham: One from among his disciples, Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, saith unto him—

text.NRSV: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish. But what are they among so many people?”
text.Rotherham: There is a little lad here, that hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes,—but, these,—what are they, for such numbers?

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Make the people sit down.” Now there was a great deal of grass in the place; so they sat down, about five thousand in all.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said—Make the people recline. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men reclined, to the number of about five thousand.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus took the loaves, and when he had given thanks, he distributed them to those who were seated; so also the fish, as much as they wanted.
text.Rotherham: Jesus therefore took the loaves, and, giving thanks, went on distributing unto them that reclined; in like manner, of the small fishes also: as much as they were wishing.

text.NRSV: When they were satisfied, he told his disciples, “Gather up the fragments left over, so that nothing may be lost.”
text.Rotherham: And, when they were well-filled, he saith unto his disciples—Gather up the broken pieces left over, that nothing be lost.

text.NRSV: So they gathered them up, and from the fragments of the five barley loaves, left by those who had eaten, they filled twelve baskets.
text.Rotherham: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets, with broken pieces out of the five barley loaves,—which were left over by them who had eaten.

text.NRSV: When the people saw the sign that he had done, they began to say, “This is indeed the prophet who is to come into the world.”
text.Rotherham: The people, therefore, seeing what signs he wrought, began to say—This, is, of a truth, the prophet who was to come into the world.

text.NRSV: When Jesus realized that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, he withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, getting to know that they were about to come, and seize him, that they might make him king, retired again into the mountain, himself, alone.

text.NRSV: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
text.Rotherham: But, when evening came, his disciples went down unto the sea;

text.NRSV: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
text.Rotherham: and, entering into a boat, were going across the sea into Capernaum. And, dark, already, had it become, and, not yet, had Jesus reached them;

text.NRSV: The sea became rough because a strong wind was blowing.
text.Rotherham: moreover the sea, by reason of a great wind that blew, was rising high.

text.NRSV: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the boat, and they were terrified.
text.Rotherham: Having therefore rowed about twenty-five or thirty furlongs, they observe Jesus, walking upon the sea, and, near the boat, coming; and they were affrighted.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, saith unto them—It is, I: be not affrighted!

text.NRSV: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the land toward which they were going.
text.Rotherham: They were willing, therefore, to receive him into the boat; and, straightway, the boat was at land, whither they had been slowly going.

text.NRSV: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there. They also saw that Jesus had not got into the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, the multitude that was standing on the other side of the sea, saw that there was not, another small-boat, there, save one,—and that Jesus entered not, along with his disciples, unto the boat, but that, alone, his disciples departed:—

text.NRSV: Then some boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
text.Rotherham: howbeit there came boats out of Tiberias, near the place where they did eat the bread, after the Lord had given thanks:—

text.NRSV: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, the multitude saw, that, Jesus, was not there, nor yet his disciples, they themselves, got into the small boats, and came unto Capernaum, seeking Jesus;

text.NRSV: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
text.Rotherham: and, finding him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him—Rabbi! when, camest thou, hither?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them, and said—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Ye seek me, not because ye saw signs, but because ye did eat of the loaves and were filled.

text.NRSV: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For it is on him that God the Father has set his seal.”
text.Rotherham: Be working, not for the food that perisheth, but for the food that endureth unto life age-abiding,—which, the Son of Man, unto you, will give; for upon, the same, hath the Father, even God, set his seal.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “What must we do to perform the works of God?”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What are we to do, that we may be working the works of God?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—This, is the work of God: that ye believe on him whom, he, hath sent forth.

text.NRSV: So they said to him, “What sign are you going to give us then, so that we may see it and believe you? What work are you performing?
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What, then, art, thou, doing, by way of sign, that we may see, and believe in thee: what art thou working?

text.NRSV: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Our fathers, did eat, the manna, in the desert,—just as it is written: Bread out of heaven, he gave them to eat.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Not Moses, gave you the bread out of heaven; but, my Father, giveth you the real bread out of heaven.

text.NRSV: For the bread of God is that which comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
text.Rotherham: For, the bread of God, is that which is coming down out of heaven, and giving, life, unto the world.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—Sir! Evermore, give us this bread.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never be hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—I, am the bread of life: he that cometh unto me, in nowise shall hunger, and, he that believeth on me, in nowise shall thirst, any more.

text.NRSV: But I said to you that you have seen me and yet do not believe.
text.Rotherham: But I told you—Ye have even seen [me], and yet do not believe.

text.NRSV: Everything that the Father gives me will come to me, and anyone who comes to me I will never drive away;
text.Rotherham: All that which the Father is giving me, unto me, will have come, and, him that cometh unto me, in nowise will I cast out,—

text.NRSV: for I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Because I have come down from heaven,—Not that I should be doing my own will, but the will of him that sent me.

text.NRSV: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I should lose nothing of all that he has given me, but raise it up on the last day.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the will of him that sent me, That, of all that which he hath given me, I should lose nothing, but should raise it up at the last day.

text.NRSV: This is indeed the will of my Father, that all who see the Son and believe in him may have eternal life; and I will raise them up on the last day.”
text.Rotherham: For, this, is the will of my Father, That, every one that vieweth the Son, and believeth on him, should have life age-abiding, and, I, should raise him up, at the last day.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews began to complain about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to murmur concerning him, because he said—I, am the bread that came down out of heaven;

text.NRSV: They were saying, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
text.Rotherham: and were saying—Is not, this, Jesus, the son of Joseph,—of whom, we, know the father and the mother! How is it then, that he, now, saith: Out of heaven, have I come down?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Do not complain among yourselves.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Be not murmuring, one with another:

text.NRSV: No one can come to me unless drawn by the Father who sent me; and I will raise that person up on the last day.
text.Rotherham: No one, can come unto me, except, the Father who sent me, draw him,—and, I, will raise him up, in the last day.

text.NRSV: It is written in the prophets, ‘And they shall all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father comes to me.
text.Rotherham: It is written in the prophets,—And they shall be, all, the instructed of God: Every one who hath heard of the Father, and learned, cometh unto me.

text.NRSV: Not that anyone has seen the Father except the one who is from God; he has seen the Father.
text.Rotherham: Not that any one hath seen, the Father, save he who is from God,—this one, hath seen the Father.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever believes has eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: He that believeth, hath life age-abiding.

text.NRSV: I am the bread of life.
text.Rotherham: I am the bread of life:—

text.NRSV: Your ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
text.Rotherham: Your fathers, did eat, in the desert, the manna,—and died:

text.NRSV: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
text.Rotherham: This, is the bread which, out of heaven, cometh down, that one, thereof, may eat,—and not die.

text.NRSV: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats of this bread will live forever; and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the living bread, which, out of heaven, came down: If one eat of this bread, he shall live unto times age-abiding; and, the bread, moreover, which, I, will give, is, my flesh—for the world’s life.

text.NRSV: The Jews then disputed among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to strive one with another, saying—How can this one, unto us, give his flesh to eat?

text.NRSV: So Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you—Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of Man, and drink his blood, ye have not life within yourselves.

text.NRSV: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood have eternal life, and I will raise them up on the last day;
text.Rotherham: He that feedeth upon my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath life age-abiding, and, I, will raise him up at the last day;

text.NRSV: for my flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
text.Rotherham: For, my flesh, is, true, food, and, my blood, is, true, drink:

text.NRSV: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood abide in me, and I in them.
text.Rotherham: He that feedeth upon my flesh, and drinketh my blood, in me, abideth, and, I, in him.

text.NRSV: Just as the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me will live because of me.
text.Rotherham: Just as the living Father sent me,—and I live by reason of the Father, he also that feedeth upon me, even he, shall live by reason of me.

text.NRSV: This is the bread that came down from heaven, not like that which your ancestors ate, and they died. But the one who eats this bread will live forever.”
text.Rotherham: This, is the bread, which, out of heaven, came down:—Not just as your fathers did eat—and died! He that feedeth upon this bread, shall live unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: He said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue at Capernaum.
text.Rotherham: These things, said he, as, in a synagogue, he was teaching, in Capernaum.

text.NRSV: When many of his disciples heard it, they said, “This teaching is difficult; who can accept it?”
text.Rotherham: Many, of his disciples, therefore, when they heard, said—Hard, is this discourse,—Who can, thereunto, hearken?

text.NRSV: But Jesus, being aware that his disciples were complaining about it, said to them, “Does this offend you?
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, knowing within himself that his disciples were murmuring concerning this, said unto them—Doth, this, cause, you, to stumble?

text.NRSV: Then what if you were to see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
text.Rotherham: [What] then, if ye should view the Son of Man ascending where he was before? …

text.NRSV: It is the spirit that gives life; the flesh is useless. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
text.Rotherham: The spirit, it is, that giveth life,—the flesh, profiteth, nothing: The declarations which, I, have spoken unto you, are, spirit, and, are, life.

text.NRSV: But among you there are some who do not believe.” For Jesus knew from the first who were the ones that did not believe, and who was the one that would betray him.
text.Rotherham: But there are some from among you, who do not believe. For Jesus knew from the beginning, who they were that did not believe, and who it was would deliver him up;—

text.NRSV: And he said, “For this reason I have told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted by the Father.”
text.Rotherham: and he went on to say—For this cause, have I said unto you: No one, can come unto me, except it have been given him, of the Father.

text.NRSV: Because of this many of his disciples turned back and no longer went about with him.
text.Rotherham: Because of this, many from among his disciples, went away back, and, no longer, with him, were walking.

text.NRSV: So Jesus asked the twelve, “Do you also wish to go away?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto the twelve—Are, ye also, wishing to withdraw?

text.NRSV: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom can we go? You have the words of eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter answered him—Lord! unto whom, shall we go? Declarations of life age-abiding, thou hast;

text.NRSV: We have come to believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.”
text.Rotherham: And, we, have believed, and come to know,—that, thou, art the Holy One of God.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—Did not, I, make choice, of you, the twelve? And yet, from among you, one, is, an adversary.

text.NRSV: He was speaking of Judas son of Simon Iscariot, for he, though one of the twelve, was going to betray him.
text.Rotherham: Now he was speaking of Judas, son of Simon Iscariot: for, the same, was about to deliver him up,—one of the twelve.

text.NRSV: After this Jesus went about in Galilee. He did not wish to go about in Judea because the Jews were looking for an opportunity to kill him.
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee; for he was not wishing, in Judaea, to be walking, because the Jews were seeking to slay him.

text.NRSV: Now the Jewish festival of Booths was near.
text.Rotherham: But the feast of the Jews was near, that of the pitching of tents.

text.NRSV: So his brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea so that your disciples also may see the works you are doing;
text.Rotherham: His brethren, therefore, said unto him—Remove hence, and withdraw into Judaea, that, thy disciples also, may view thy works which thou art doing.

text.NRSV: for no one who wants to be widely known acts in secret. If you do these things, show yourself to the world.”
text.Rotherham: For, no one, anything in secret, doeth, and yet is seeking, himself, to be well-known. If, these things, thou art doing, manifest thyself unto the world.

text.NRSV: (For not even his brothers believed in him.)
text.Rotherham: For, not even his brethren, were believing on him.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always here.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, saith unto them—My season, not yet, is here; but, your season, at anytime, is ready:

text.NRSV: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify against it that its works are evil.
text.Rotherham: The world cannot hate you; but, me, it hateth, because, I, bear witness concerning it, that, the works thereof, are, wicked.

text.NRSV: Go to the festival yourselves. I am not going to this festival, for my time has not yet fully come.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, go up unto the feast: I, not yet, am coming up unto this feast, because, my season, not yet, hath fully come.

text.NRSV: After saying this, he remained in Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, having said unto them, he was still abiding in Galilee.

text.NRSV: But after his brothers had gone to the festival, then he also went, not publicly but as it were in secret.
text.Rotherham: But, when his brethren had gone up unto the feast, then, he also, went up,—not openly, but as it were in secret.

text.NRSV: The Jews were looking for him at the festival and saying, “Where is he?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were seeking him in the feast, and were saying—Where is that one?

text.NRSV: And there was considerable complaining about him among the crowds. While some said, “He is a good man,” others said, “No, he is deceiving the crowd.”
text.Rotherham: and there was great, murmuring, about him among the multitudes: some, indeed, were saying—Good, is he! [but], others, were saying—Not so! but he is leading the multitude astray.

text.NRSV: Yet no one would speak openly about him for fear of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: No one, nevertheless, was speaking, openly, about him, because of their fear of the Jews.

text.NRSV: About the middle of the festival Jesus went up into the temple and began to teach.
text.Rotherham: Presently, however, the feast being at its height, Jesus went up unto the temple and was teaching.

text.NRSV: The Jews were astonished at it, saying, “How does this man have such learning, when he has never been taught?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to marvel, saying—How, doth this one know, letters, not having learned?

text.NRSV: Then Jesus answered them, “My teaching is not mine but his who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, answered them, and said—My teaching, is not mine, but his who sent me.

text.NRSV: Anyone who resolves to do the will of God will know whether the teaching is from God or whether I am speaking on my own.
text.Rotherham: If any one intend, his will, to do, he shall get to know concerning the teaching—whether it is, of God, or, I, from myself, am speaking,

text.NRSV: Those who speak on their own seek their own glory; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and there is nothing false in him.
text.Rotherham: He that, from himself, doth speak, his own glory, is seeking: he that seeketh the glory of him that sent him, the same, is, true, and, injustice, in him, is there none.

text.NRSV: “Did not Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you looking for an opportunity to kill me?”
text.Rotherham: Did not, Moses, give you the law? And yet, not one of you, doeth the law!—Why, seek ye, to slay, me?

text.NRSV: The crowd answered, “You have a demon! Who is trying to kill you?”
text.Rotherham: The multitude answered—A demon, thou hast: Who, is seeking, to slay, thee?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “I performed one work, and all of you are astonished.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—One work, I did, and ye, all, are marvelling:

text.NRSV: Moses gave you circumcision (it is, of course, not from Moses, but from the patriarchs), and you circumcise a man on the sabbath.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, Moses, hath given you, circumcision,—not that, of Moses, it is, but of the fathers;—and, on Sabbath, ye circumcise a man.

text.NRSV: If a man receives circumcision on the sabbath in order that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me because I healed a man’s whole body on the sabbath?
text.Rotherham: If, circumcision, a man receiveth, on Sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken, with me, are ye bitter as gall, because, a whole man, I made, well, on Sabbath?

text.NRSV: Do not judge by appearances, but judge with right judgment.”
text.Rotherham: Be not judging according to appearance, but, just judgment, be judging,

text.NRSV: Now some of the people of Jerusalem were saying, “Is not this the man they are trying to kill?
text.Rotherham: So then, some of them of Jerusalem were saying—Is not, this, he whom they are seeking to kill?

text.NRSV: And here he is, speaking openly, but they say nothing to him! Can it be that the authorities really know that this is the Messiah?
text.Rotherham: And yet, see! with freedom of speech, he is talking, and, nothing, unto him, do they say:—Have the rulers perhaps come to know, of a truth, that, this, is, the Christ?

text.NRSV: Yet we know where this man is from; but when the Messiah comes, no one will know where he is from.”
text.Rotherham: But, as for this one, we know whence he is; The Christ, however, whensoever he shall come, no one, getteth to know whence he is.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus cried out as he was teaching in the temple, “You know me, and you know where I am from. I have not come on my own. But the one who sent me is true, and you do not know him.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, cried aloud in the temple, teaching, and saying—Both me, ye know, and ye know whence I am,—and yet, of myself, have I not come, but he is real, who sent me, whom, ye, know not.

text.NRSV: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me.”
text.Rotherham: I, know him, because, from him, I am, and, he, sent me forth.

text.NRSV: Then they tried to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him, because his hour had not yet come.
text.Rotherham: They were seeking therefore to seize him; and yet no one laid on him his hand, because, not yet, had come his hour.

text.NRSV: Yet many in the crowd believed in him and were saying, “When the Messiah comes, will he do more signs than this man has done?”
text.Rotherham: But, from amongst the multitude, many, believed on him, and were saying—The Christ, whensoever he cometh, greater signs, will he do, than those which, this one, did?

text.NRSV: The Pharisees heard the crowd muttering such things about him, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent temple police to arrest him.
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees hearkened unto the multitude murmuring concerning him these things; and the High-priests and the Pharisees sent forth officers, that they might seize him.

text.NRSV: Jesus then said, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I am going to him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Yet a little time, I am, with you, and withdraw unto him that sent me:

text.NRSV: You will search for me, but you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot come.”
text.Rotherham: Ye shall seek me, and not find me, and, where, I, am, ye, cannot come.

text.NRSV: The Jews said to one another, “Where does this man intend to go that we will not find him? Does he intend to go to the Dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks?
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said unto themselves—Whither, is, this one, about to go, that we shall not find him? Unto the dispersion of the Greeks, is he about to go, and teach the Greeks?

text.NRSV: What does he mean by saying, ‘You will search for me and you will not find me’ and ‘Where I am, you cannot come’?”
text.Rotherham: What, is this which he said: Ye shall seek me and not find me, and, where, I, am, ye, cannot come?

text.NRSV: On the last day of the festival, the great day, while Jesus was standing there, he cried out, “Let anyone who is thirsty come to me,
text.Rotherham: Now, on the last—the great—day of the feast, Jesus was standing, and he cried aloud, saying—If any man thirst, let him come unto me and drink:

text.NRSV: and let the one who believes in me drink. As the scripture has said, ‘Out of the believer’s heart shall flow rivers of living water.’ ”
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on me—just as said the Scripture,—River, from within him, shall flow, of living water.

text.NRSV: Now he said this about the Spirit, which believers in him were to receive; for as yet there was no Spirit, because Jesus was not yet glorified.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, spake he concerning the Spirit which they who believed on him were about to receive; for, not yet, was there Spirit, because, Jesus, not yet, was glorified!

text.NRSV: When they heard these words, some in the crowd said, “This is really the prophet.”
text.Rotherham: Some from amongst the multitude, therefore, having hearkened unto these words, were saying—This, is, in truth, the prophet.

text.NRSV: Others said, “This is the Messiah.” But some asked, “Surely the Messiah does not come from Galilee, does he?
text.Rotherham: Others, were saying—This, is, the Christ. But, others, were saying—Out of Galilee, is, the Christ, to come?

text.NRSV: Has not the scripture said that the Messiah is descended from David and comes from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?”
text.Rotherham: Hath not the Scripture said: Of the seed of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David was, cometh the Christ?

text.NRSV: So there was a division in the crowd because of him.
text.Rotherham: A division, therefore, arose in the multitude, because of him.

text.NRSV: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him.
text.Rotherham: Certain, indeed, from among them, were wishing to seize him; but, no one, thrust upon him his hands.

text.NRSV: Then the temple police went back to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why did you not arrest him?”
text.Rotherham: So then the officers came unto the High-priests and Pharisees, and, they, said unto them—For what cause, have ye not brought him?

text.NRSV: The police answered, “Never has anyone spoken like this!”
text.Rotherham: The officers answered—Never, man spake thus!

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees replied, “Surely you have not been deceived too, have you?
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees, therefore, answered them—Surely, ye also, have not been led astray?

text.NRSV: Has any one of the authorities or of the Pharisees believed in him?
text.Rotherham: Surely, none of the rulers, hath believed in him, nor of the Pharisees?

text.NRSV: But this crowd, which does not know the law—they are accursed.”
text.Rotherham: But, this multitude, that take no note of the law, are, laid under a curse.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus before, and who was one of them, asked,
text.Rotherham: Nicodemus saith unto them—he that came unto him formerly, being, one, from among them—

text.NRSV: “Our law does not judge people without first giving them a hearing to find out what they are doing, does it?”
text.Rotherham: Surely, our law, doth not judge the man, unless it hear first from him, and get to know what he is doing?

text.NRSV: They replied, “Surely you are not also from Galilee, are you? Search and you will see that no prophet is to arise from Galilee.”
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—Surely, thou also, art not, of Galilee? Search and see! That, out of Galilee, a prophet is not to arise.

text.NRSV: Then each of them went home,
text.Rotherham: [[And they went, each unto his own house;

text.NRSV: while Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
text.Rotherham: but, Jesus, went unto the Mount of Olives.

text.NRSV: Early in the morning he came again to the temple. All the people came to him and he sat down and began to teach them.
text.Rotherham: And, early in the morning, again, came he into the temple, and all the people were coming unto him; and, sitting down, he began to teach them.

text.NRSV: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery; and making her stand before all of them,
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and the Pharisees bring a woman, caught, in adultery; and, setting her in the midst,

text.NRSV: they said to him, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the very act of committing adultery.
text.Rotherham: they say unto him—Teacher! this woman, hath been caught in the very act of committing adultery!

text.NRSV: Now in the law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?”
text.Rotherham: Now, in the law, Moses, [unto us] gave command to stone, such as these. What, then, dost, thou, say?

text.NRSV: They said this to test him, so that they might have some charge to bring against him. Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.
text.Rotherham: [This, however, they were saying, by way of testing him,—that they might have whereof to accuse him.] But, Jesus, stooping down, with his finger, wrote in the ground.

text.NRSV: When they kept on questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, “Let anyone among you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her.”
text.Rotherham: When, however, they still continued questioning him, he lifted himself up and said [unto them]—He of you, that is without sin, let him first cast at her a stone;

text.NRSV: And once again he bent down and wrote on the ground.
text.Rotherham: and, again stooping down, he wrote in the ground.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they went away, one by one, beginning with the elders; and Jesus was left alone with the woman standing before him.
text.Rotherham: And, they who heard, began to go out, one by one, beginning from the elders,—and he was left, alone; the woman also, being, in the midst.

text.NRSV: Jesus straightened up and said to her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
text.Rotherham: And lifting himself up, Jesus said unto her—Woman, where are they? Hath, no one, condemned thee?

text.NRSV: She said, “No one, sir.” And Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go your way, and from now on do not sin again.”
text.Rotherham: And, she, said—No one, Sir! And Jesus said—Neither do, I, condemn thee,—be going thy way: Henceforth, be sinning, no more.]]

text.NRSV: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness but will have the light of life.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, unto them, spake Jesus, saying—I, am the light of the world: He that followeth me, shall in nowise walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees said to him, “You are testifying on your own behalf; your testimony is not valid.”
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees, therefore, said unto him—Thou, concerning thyself, bearest witness: thy witness, is not true.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Even if I testify on my own behalf, my testimony is valid because I know where I have come from and where I am going, but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Even though, I, bear witness concerning myself, true, is my witness, because I know whence I came, and whither I go; but, ye, know not whence I come, and whither I go.

text.NRSV: You judge by human standards; I judge no one.
text.Rotherham: Ye, according to the flesh, do judge: I, am judging no one.

text.NRSV: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is valid; for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.
