text.Rotherham: And, even if, I, am judging, my judging, is, genuine,—because, alone, am I not, but, I, and the Father who sent me;

text.NRSV: In your law it is written that the testimony of two witnesses is valid.
text.Rotherham: And, in your own law, it is written, that, two men’s witness, is, true:

text.NRSV: I testify on my own behalf, and the Father who sent me testifies on my behalf.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the one bearing witness of myself, and the Father who sent me is bearing witness concerning me.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
text.Rotherham: They were saying unto him, therefore—Where, is thy father? Jesus answered—Neither, me, do ye know, nor yet my Father: If, me, ye had known, my Father also, had ye known.

text.NRSV: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the treasury of the temple, but no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
text.Rotherham: These sayings, spake he in the treasury, teaching in the temple, and yet, no one, seized him, because, not yet, had come his hour.

text.NRSV: Again he said to them, “I am going away, and you will search for me, but you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.”
text.Rotherham: He said unto them again, therefore—I, go, and ye shall seek me,—and yet, in your sin, shall ye die: Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews said, “Is he going to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come’?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were saying—Can it be that he will kill himself, that he saith, Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You are from below, I am from above; you are of this world, I am not of this world.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—Ye, are, of the realms below, I, am, of the realms above: Ye, are, of this world, I, am not of this world.

text.NRSV: I told you that you would die in your sins, for you will die in your sins unless you believe that I am he.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore said I unto you, Ye shall die in your sins: For, if ye believe not that, I, am he, ye shall die in your sins.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Why do I speak to you at all?
text.Rotherham: They were saying unto him, therefore—Who art, thou? Jesus said unto them—First and foremost, even what I speak unto you.

text.NRSV: I have much to say about you and much to condemn; but the one who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him.”
text.Rotherham: Many things, have I, concerning you, to speak, and to judge; but, he who sent me, is, true, and, I, what things I heard from him, the same, speak I unto the world.

text.NRSV: They did not understand that he was speaking to them about the Father.
text.Rotherham: They noted not that, as touching the Father, unto them, he was speaking.

text.NRSV: So Jesus said, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will realize that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own, but I speak these things as the Father instructed me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Whensoever ye shall lift up the Son of Man, then, shall ye know, that, I, am he, and, of myself, am doing, nothing; but, just as the Father taught me, the same things, am I speaking.

text.NRSV: And the one who sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, for I always do what is pleasing to him.”
text.Rotherham: And, he that sent me, is, with me: He hath not left me, alone. Because, I, the things that please him, ever do.

text.NRSV: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
text.Rotherham: As he was speaking these things, many, believed on him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him, “If you continue in my word, you are truly my disciples;
text.Rotherham: Jesus was saying, therefore, unto the Jews who had believed on him—If, ye, abide in my word, of a truth, my disciples, ye are;

text.NRSV: and you will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.”
text.Rotherham: And ye shall know the truth, and, the truth, shall make you free.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “We are descendants of Abraham and have never been slaves to anyone. What do you mean by saying, ‘You will be made free’?”
text.Rotherham: They made answer unto him—Seed of Abraham, are we, and, unto no one, have been brought into bondage, at any time: How sayest, thou, Free, shall ye be made?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave to sin.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you: Every one who committeth sin, is, a slave, [of sin:]

text.NRSV: The slave does not have a permanent place in the household; the son has a place there forever.
text.Rotherham: Now, the slave, doth not abide in the house, evermore. The Son, abideth, evermore.

text.NRSV: So if the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
text.Rotherham: If then, the Son, shall make you free, really free, shall ye be.

text.NRSV: I know that you are descendants of Abraham; yet you look for an opportunity to kill me, because there is no place in you for my word.
text.Rotherham: I know that ye are, seed of Abraham; but ye are seeking to kill me, because, my word, findeth no place in you.

text.NRSV: I declare what I have seen in the Father’s presence; as for you, you should do what you have heard from your father.”
text.Rotherham: What things, I, have seen with the Father, I am speaking; Ye, also, then, what things ye have heard from your father, are doing.—

text.NRSV: They answered him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would be doing what Abraham did,
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—Our father, is, Abraham. Jesus saith unto them—If, children of Abraham, ye are, the works of Abraham, are ye doing.

text.NRSV: but now you are trying to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. This is not what Abraham did.
text.Rotherham: But, now, ye are seeking, to kill me,—A man who, the truth, unto you, hath spoken, which I have heard from God: this, Abraham, did not.

text.NRSV: You are indeed doing what your father does.” They said to him, “We are not illegitimate children; we have one father, God himself.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, are doing the works of your father. They said unto him—We, of fornication, were not born: One father, have we—God.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and now I am here. I did not come on my own, but he sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—If, God, had been your father, ye had been loving me, for, I, from God, came forth, and am here; for, not even of myself, have I come, but, he, sent me forth.

text.NRSV: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot accept my word.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, is it, that, my speech, ye do not understand? because ye cannot hear my word.

text.NRSV: You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father’s desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks according to his own nature, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are, of your father—the adversary, and, the covetings of your father, ye choose to be doing. He, was, a murderer, from the beginning, and, in the truth, he stood not; because truth is not in him: Whensoever he speaketh falsehood, of his own, he speaketh; because, false, he is, and, the father of it.

text.NRSV: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
text.Rotherham: But, as for me, because, the truth, I speak, ye do not believe me.

text.NRSV: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
text.Rotherham: Which of you convicteth me of sin? If, truth, I speak, wherefore, do, ye, not believe me?

text.NRSV: Whoever is from God hears the words of God. The reason you do not hear them is that you are not from God.”
text.Rotherham: He that is of God, heareth, the sayings of God; therefore, do, ye, not hear, because, of God, ye are not.

text.NRSV: The Jews answered him, “Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews answered, and said unto him—Do, we, not, well, say: Thou, art, a Samaritan, and hast a demon?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon; but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—I, have not, a demon, but honour my Father: and, ye, dishonour me.

text.NRSV: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it and he is the judge.
text.Rotherham: But, I, seek not my glory: There is one who seeketh and judgeth.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever keeps my word will never see death.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: If anyone shall keep, my word, death, shall he not see, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: The Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, and so did the prophets; yet you say, ‘Whoever keeps my word will never taste death.’
text.Rotherham: The Jews said unto him—Now, we know that, a demon, thou hast:—Abraham, died, and, the prophets, and yet, thou, sayest: If anyone shall keep, my word, in nowise shall he taste of death, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets also died. Who do you claim to be?”
text.Rotherham: Surely, thou, art not, greater, than our father Abraham,—who, indeed, died? and, the prophets, died:—Whom, makest thou thyself?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, he of whom you say, ‘He is our God,’
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—If, I, glorify myself, my glory, is nothing: It is, my Father, that glorifieth me,—of whom, ye, say—He is, your God!

text.NRSV: though you do not know him. But I know him; if I would say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know him and I keep his word.
text.Rotherham: And yet ye have not come to know him; but, I, do know him: If I say, I know him not, I shall be like you—false; but I know him, and, his word, am I keeping.

text.NRSV: Your ancestor Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day; he saw it and was glad.”
text.Rotherham: Abraham, your father, exulted that he should see my day; and he saw, and rejoiced.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said unto him—Fifty years old, not yet art thou, and, Abraham, hast thou seen?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, before Abraham was, I am.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Before, Abraham, came into existence, I, am.

text.NRSV: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.
text.Rotherham: They took up stones, therefore, that they might cast at him; but, Jesus, was hidden, and went forth out of the temple.

text.NRSV: As he walked along, he saw a man blind from birth.
text.Rotherham: And, passing along, he saw a man, blind from birth.

text.NRSV: His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
text.Rotherham: And his disciples questioned him, saying—Rabbi! who sinned, this man or his parents, that, blind, he should be born?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Neither this man nor his parents sinned; he was born blind so that God’s works might be revealed in him.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Neither, this man, sinned nor his parents; but…that the works of God should be made manifest in him.

text.NRSV: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming when no one can work.
text.Rotherham: We must needs be working the works of him that sent me, while it is, day: There cometh a night, when, no one, can work.

text.NRSV: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
text.Rotherham: Whensoever I may be, in the world, I am, the light, of the world.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he spat on the ground and made mud with the saliva and spread the mud on the man’s eyes,
text.Rotherham: These things, having said, he spat on the ground, and made clay with the spittle, and laid the clay upon his eyes;

text.NRSV: saying to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means Sent). Then he went and washed and came back able to see.
text.Rotherham: and said unto him—Withdraw! wash in the pool of Siloam,—which is to be translated, Sent. He went away, therefore, and washed, and came, seeing.

text.NRSV: The neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar began to ask, “Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?”
text.Rotherham: The neighbours, therefore, and they who used to observe him aforetime—that he was, a beggar, were saying—Is not, this, he that used to sit and beg?

text.NRSV: Some were saying, “It is he.” Others were saying, “No, but it is someone like him.” He kept saying, “I am the man.”
text.Rotherham: Others, were saying—’Tis, the same. Others, were saying—Nay! but he is, like him. He, was saying—I, am he.

text.NRSV: But they kept asking him, “Then how were your eyes opened?”
text.Rotherham: So they were saying unto him—How [then] were thine eyes opened?

text.NRSV: He answered, “The man called Jesus made mud, spread it on my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ Then I went and washed and received my sight.”
text.Rotherham: He, answered—The man that is called Jesus, made, clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me: Withdraw unto the pool of Siloam, and wash. Going away, therefore, and washing, I received sight.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Where is he?” He said, “I do not know.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto him—Where is, He? He saith—I know not.

text.NRSV: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.
text.Rotherham: They bring him unto the Pharisees—him at one time blind.

text.NRSV: Now it was a sabbath day when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Now it was Sabbath, on the day when Jesus made, the clay, and opened his eyes.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees also began to ask him how he had received his sight. He said to them, “He put mud on my eyes. Then I washed, and now I see.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, the Pharisees also questioned him, as to how he received sight. And, he, said unto them—Clay, laid he upon mine eyes, and I washed,—and do see.

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, for he does not observe the sabbath.” But others said, “How can a man who is a sinner perform such signs?” And they were divided.
text.Rotherham: Certain from among the Pharisees, therefore, were saying—This man is not, from God, because, the Sabbath, he keepeth not. Others, [however] were saying—How can a sinful man, such signs as these, be doing? And there was, a division, among them.

text.NRSV: So they said again to the blind man, “What do you say about him? It was your eyes he opened.” He said, “He is a prophet.”
text.Rotherham: So they were saying unto the blind man, again, What dost, thou, say concerning him, in that he opened thine eyes? And, he, said—A prophet, is he.

text.NRSV: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, did not believe, concerning him, that he was blind, and received sight,—until they called the parents of him that had received sight,

text.NRSV: and asked them, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
text.Rotherham: and questioned them, saying—Is, this, your son, of whom, ye, say, that blind, he was, born? How, then, seeth he, even now?

text.NRSV: His parents answered, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
text.Rotherham: His parents, therefore, answered, and said—We know that, this, is our son, and that, blind, he was born;

text.NRSV: but we do not know how it is that now he sees, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he is of age. He will speak for himself.”
text.Rotherham: But, how he now seeth, we know not, or, who opened his eyes, we, know not,—Question, him, he is, of age, he, concerning himself, shall speak.

text.NRSV: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that anyone who confessed Jesus to be the Messiah would be put out of the synagogue.
text.Rotherham: These things, said his parents, because they were in fear of the Jews,—for, already, had the Jews agreed together, that, if anyone should confess, him, to be Christ, an, excommunicant from the synagogue, should he be made.

text.NRSV: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
text.Rotherham: For this cause, his parents said—He is, of age,—question him.

text.NRSV: So for the second time they called the man who had been blind, and they said to him, “Give glory to God! We know that this man is a sinner.”
text.Rotherham: So they called the man a second time—[him] who had been blind, and said unto him—Give glory unto God! We know that, this man, is, a sinner.

text.NRSV: He answered, “I do not know whether he is a sinner. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see.”
text.Rotherham: He, therefore, answered—Whether he is a sinner, I know not: One thing, I know,—That, whereas I was, blind, now, I see!

text.NRSV: They said to him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What did he unto thee? How opened he thine eyes?

text.NRSV: He answered them, “I have told you already, and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text.Rotherham: He answered them—I told you just now, and ye did not hear: Why, again, do ye wish to hear? Are, ye also, wishing to become, his disciples?

text.NRSV: Then they reviled him, saying, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
text.Rotherham: And they reviled him, and said—Thou, art, the disciple, of that man; but, we, are, Moses’, disciples:—

text.NRSV: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he comes from.”
text.Rotherham: We, know, that, unto Moses, hath God spoken; but, as for this man, we know not whence he is.

text.NRSV: The man answered, “Here is an astonishing thing! You do not know where he comes from, and yet he opened my eyes.
text.Rotherham: The man answered, and said unto them—Why! Herein, is, the marvel: That, ye, know not whence he is, and yet he opened mine eyes.

text.NRSV: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but he does listen to one who worships him and obeys his will.
text.Rotherham: We know that, God, unto sinners, doth not hearken: but, if one be, a worshipper of God, and be doing, his will, unto this one, he hearkeneth.

text.NRSV: Never since the world began has it been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a person born blind.
text.Rotherham: Out of age-past time, hath it never been heard, that anyone opened the eyes of one who, blind, had been born.

text.NRSV: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”
text.Rotherham: If this man were not from God, he could have done nothing.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “You were born entirely in sins, and are you trying to teach us?” And they drove him out.
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—In sins, wast, thou, born, altogether; and art, thou, teaching, us? And they cast him out.

text.NRSV: Jesus heard that they had driven him out, and when he found him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus heard that they had cast him out: and, finding him, said—Dost, thou, believe on the son of Man?

text.NRSV: He answered, “And who is he, sir? Tell me, so that I may believe in him.”
text.Rotherham: He answered [and said]—And, who, is he, Sir, that I may believe on him?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “You have seen him, and the one speaking with you is he.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto him—Thou hast both seen him and, he that is speaking with thee, is, he.

text.NRSV: He said, “Lord, I believe.” And he worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—I believe, Sir! and worshipped him.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment so that those who do not see may see, and those who do see may become blind.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said—For judgment, I, unto this world, came: that, they who were not seeing, might see, and, they who were seeing, might become, blind.

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees near him heard this and said to him, “Surely we are not blind, are we?”
text.Rotherham: They of the Pharisees who were with him, heard, these things, and said unto him—Are, we also, blind?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would not have sin. But now that you say, ‘We see,’ your sin remains.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—If, blind, ye had been, ye had not had sin; but, now, ye say, We see, your sin, abideth.

text.NRSV: “Very truly, I tell you, anyone who does not enter the sheepfold by the gate but climbs in by another way is a thief and a bandit.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you:—He that entereth not, through the door, into the fold of the sheep, but goeth up from another place, that man, is, a thief, and, a robber.

text.NRSV: The one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
text.Rotherham: But, he that entereth through the door, is, shepherd, of the sheep:

text.NRSV: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep hear his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
text.Rotherham: To him, the porter openeth, and, the sheep, unto his voice, hearken: and, his own sheep, he calleth by name, and leadeth them forth.

text.NRSV: When he has brought out all his own, he goes ahead of them, and the sheep follow him because they know his voice.
text.Rotherham: As soon as, all his own, he hath put forth, before them, he moveth on, and, the sheep, follow him, because they know his voice;

text.NRSV: They will not follow a stranger, but they will run from him because they do not know the voice of strangers.”
text.Rotherham: But, a stranger, will they in nowise follow, but will flee from him, because they know not the voice, of strangers.

text.NRSV: Jesus used this figure of speech with them, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.
text.Rotherham: This similitude, spake Jesus unto them; but, those men, understood not what the things were which he was speaking unto them.

text.NRSV: So again Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said, again—Verily, verily, I say unto you:—I, am the door of the sheep:

text.NRSV: All who came before me are thieves and bandits; but the sheep did not listen to them.
text.Rotherham: All, as many as came before me, are thieves, and, robbers: but the sheep hearkened nor unto them.

text.NRSV: I am the gate. Whoever enters by me will be saved, and will come in and go out and find pasture.
text.Rotherham: I, am the door: through me, if anyone enter, he shall be saved, and shall come in and go out, and, pasture, shall find.

text.NRSV: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life, and have it abundantly.
text.Rotherham: The thief, cometh not, save that he may thieve and slay and destroy: I, came, that, life, they might have, and, above measure, might have.

text.NRSV: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: I, am the good shepherd: The good shepherd, his life, layeth down, for the sheep.

text.NRSV: The hired hand, who is not the shepherd and does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and runs away—and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.
text.Rotherham: The hireling, even because he is no shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, vieweth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth,—and, the wolf, seizeth them and scattereth,—

text.NRSV: The hired hand runs away because a hired hand does not care for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: Because, a hireling, he is, and hath no care for the sheep.

text.NRSV: I am the good shepherd. I know my own and my own know me,
text.Rotherham: I, am the good shepherd, and know my own, and, my own, know me,—

text.NRSV: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. And I lay down my life for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: Just as, the Father, knoweth me, and, I, know, the Father; and, my life, I lay down for the sheep.

text.NRSV: I have other sheep that do not belong to this fold. I must bring them also, and they will listen to my voice. So there will be one flock, one shepherd.
text.Rotherham: And, other sheep, have I, which are not of this fold: those also, I must needs bring, and, unto my voice, will they hearken, and there shall come to be, One flock, One shepherd.

text.NRSV: For this reason the Father loves me, because I lay down my life in order to take it up again.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, doth the Father, love, me, because, I, lay down my life, that, again, I may receive it:—

text.NRSV: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it up again. I have received this command from my Father.”
text.Rotherham: No one, forced it from me, but, I, lay it down, of myself,—Authority, have I, to lay it down, and, authority, have I, again, to receive it: This commandment, received I, from my Father.

text.NRSV: Again the Jews were divided because of these words.
text.Rotherham: A division, again, took place among the Jews, because of these words.

text.NRSV: Many of them were saying, “He has a demon and is out of his mind. Why listen to him?”
text.Rotherham: But many from among them were saying—A demon, he hath, and is raving,—Why, unto him, do ye hearken?

text.NRSV: Others were saying, “These are not the words of one who has a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
text.Rotherham: Others, said—These sayings, are not those of one demonized,—Can, a demon, open the eyes of, the blind?

text.NRSV: At that time the festival of the Dedication took place in Jerusalem. It was winter,
text.Rotherham: The feast of dedication took place at that time, in Jerusalem: it was, winter,

text.NRSV: and Jesus was walking in the temple, in the portico of Solomon.
text.Rotherham: and Jesus was walking in the temple, in the porch of Solomon.

text.NRSV: So the Jews gathered around him and said to him, “How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, surrounded him, and were saying unto him—How long, holdest thou, our lives, in suspense? If, thou, art the Christ, tell us, plainly.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I have told you, and you do not believe. The works that I do in my Father’s name testify to me;
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—I told you, and ye believe not: The works which, I, am doing in the name of my Father, the same, bear witness concerning me.

text.NRSV: but you do not believe, because you do not belong to my sheep.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, believe not, because ye are not of my sheep.

text.NRSV: My sheep hear my voice. I know them, and they follow me.
text.Rotherham: My sheep, unto my voice, hearken,—and, I, know, them, and they follow me,—

text.NRSV: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one will snatch them out of my hand.
text.Rotherham: And, I, give unto them life age-abiding, and in nowise shall they perish, unto times age-abiding; and no one shall carry them off out of my hand.

text.NRSV: What my Father has given me is greater than all else, and no one can snatch it out of the Father’s hand.
text.Rotherham: What, my Father, hath given me, is, something greater than all, and, no one, can carry off out of the hand of my Father:—

text.NRSV: The Father and I are one.”
text.Rotherham: I and the Father, are, one.

text.NRSV: The Jews again took up stones to stone him.
text.Rotherham: The Jews again lifted up stones, that they might stone him.

text.NRSV: Jesus replied, “I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of these are you going to stone me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—Many works, have I showed you, noble ones, from my Father: For which of those works are ye stoning me?

text.NRSV: The Jews answered, “It is not for a good work that we are going to stone you, but for blasphemy, because you, though only a human being, are making yourself God.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews answered him—Concerning a noble work, are we not stoning thee; but concerning profane speech,—and because, thou, being, a man, art making thyself, God.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Is it not written in your law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—Is it not written in your law: I, said, Ye are, gods?

text.NRSV: If those to whom the word of God came were called ‘gods’—and the scripture cannot be annulled—
text.Rotherham: If, those, he called gods, unto whom, the word of God, came—and the Scripture cannot be broken—

text.NRSV: can you say that the one whom the Father has sanctified and sent into the world is blaspheming because I said, ‘I am God’s Son’?
text.Rotherham: Of him whom, the Father, hallowed and sent forth into the world, are, ye, saying—Thou speakest profanely, because I said, Son of God, I am?

text.NRSV: If I am not doing the works of my Father, then do not believe me.
text.Rotherham: If I am not doing the works of my Father, do not believe in me;

text.NRSV: But if I do them, even though you do not believe me, believe the works, so that you may know and understand that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.”
text.Rotherham: But, if I am doing them, even though, in me, ye believe not, in the works, believe,—that ye may get to know and go on to know, that the Father is, in me, and, I, am, in the Father.

text.NRSV: Then they tried to arrest him again, but he escaped from their hands.
text.Rotherham: They were [therefore] again seeking to take him; and he went forth out of their hand.

text.NRSV: He went away again across the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing earlier, and he remained there.
text.Rotherham: And he went away again, beyond the Jordan, unto the place where John was at the first, immersing; and he abode there.

text.NRSV: Many came to him, and they were saying, “John performed no sign, but everything that John said about this man was true.”
text.Rotherham: And, many, came unto him, and were saying—John, indeed, did not so much as, one sign; but, all things, whatsoever John said concerning this one, were, true.

text.NRSV: And many believed in him there.
text.Rotherham: And, many, believed on him there.

text.NRSV: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
text.Rotherham: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus of Bethany, of the village of Mary and Martha her sister.

text.NRSV: Mary was the one who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair; her brother Lazarus was ill.
text.Rotherham: And Mary was she who anointed the Lord with perfume, and wiped his feet with her hair—whose brother Lazarus was sick.

text.NRSV: So the sisters sent a message to Jesus, “Lord, he whom you love is ill.”
text.Rotherham: The sisters, therefore, sent out unto him, saying—Lord, see! he whom thou tenderly lovest, is sick.

text.NRSV: But when Jesus heard it, he said, “This illness does not lead to death; rather it is for God’s glory, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus hearing, said—This sickness, is not unto death, but for the glory of God,—that the Son of God may be glorified thereby.

text.NRSV: Accordingly, though Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus,
text.Rotherham: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

text.NRSV: after having heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed two days longer in the place where he was.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he heard that he was sick, then, indeed, he abode in the place where he was, two days.

text.NRSV: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
text.Rotherham: Then, after this, he saith unto the disciples—Let us be leading on into Judaea, again.

text.NRSV: The disciples said to him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just now trying to stone you, and are you going there again?”
text.Rotherham: The disciples say unto him—Rabbi! just now, were the Jews seeking to stone thee—and, again, goest thou thither?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours of daylight? Those who walk during the day do not stumble, because they see the light of this world.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Are there not, twelve hours, in the day? If one walk in the day, he doth no stumble, because, the light of this world, he seeth;

text.NRSV: But those who walk at night stumble, because the light is not in them.”
text.Rotherham: But, if one walk in the night, he stumbleth, because, the light, is not in him.

text.NRSV: After saying this, he told them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I am going there to awaken him.”
text.Rotherham: These things, he said, and, after this, he saith unto them—Lazarus, our dear friend, hath fallen asleep; but I am going, that I may awake him.

text.NRSV: The disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will be all right.”
text.Rotherham: The disciples, therefore, said unto him—Lord! if he have fallen asleep, he will recover.

text.NRSV: Jesus, however, had been speaking about his death, but they thought that he was referring merely to sleep.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus had spoken concerning his death; whereas, they, supposed that, concerning the taking of rest in sleep, he had been speaking.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus told them plainly, “Lazarus is dead.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, then, said unto them, plainly,—Lazarus, died;

text.NRSV: For your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
text.Rotherham: and I rejoice, for your sake—that ye may believe,—that I was not there; but let us be going unto him.

text.NRSV: Thomas, who was called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
text.Rotherham: Thomas, therefore, the one called Didymus, said unto his fellow-disciples—Let, us also, be going, that we may die with him.

text.NRSV: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, coming, found that, four days already, had he been in the tomb.

text.NRSV: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, some two miles away,
text.Rotherham: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off;

text.NRSV: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to console them about their brother.
text.Rotherham: and, many from among the Jews, had come unto Martha and Mary, that they might console them concerning their brother.

text.NRSV: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, while Mary stayed at home.
text.Rotherham: Martha, therefore, when she heard that Jesus was coming, went to meet him; but, Mary, in the house, remained sitting.

text.NRSV: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
text.Rotherham: Martha, therefore, said unto Jesus—Lord! if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died;

text.NRSV: But even now I know that God will give you whatever you ask of him.”
text.Rotherham: And, now, I know, that, whatsoever thou shalt ask of God, God, will give unto thee.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Thy brother shall rise.

text.NRSV: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day.”
text.Rotherham: Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise, in the resurrection, in the last day.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. Those who believe in me, even though they die, will live,
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto her—I, am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth on me, even though he die, shall live again!

text.NRSV: and everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”
text.Rotherham: And, no one who liveth again and believeth on me, shall in anywise die, unto times age-abiding. Believest thou this?

text.NRSV: She said to him, “Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Messiah, the Son of God, the one coming into the world.”
text.Rotherham: She saith unto him—Yea, Lord! I, have believed, that, thou, art the Christ, the Son of God,—He who, into the world, should come.

text.NRSV: When she had said this, she went back and called her sister Mary, and told her privately, “The Teacher is here and is calling for you.”
text.Rotherham: And this saying, she went away, and called Mary her sister, secretly, saying—The teacher, is present, and calleth thee.

text.NRSV: And when she heard it, she got up quickly and went to him.
text.Rotherham: And, she, when she heard, was roused up quickly, and was coming unto him.

text.NRSV: Now Jesus had not yet come to the village, but was still at the place where Martha had met him.
text.Rotherham: Not yet, however, had Jesus come into the village, but was still in the place where, Martha, met him.

text.NRSV: The Jews who were with her in the house, consoling her, saw Mary get up quickly and go out. They followed her because they thought that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, who were with her in the house and consoling her, seeing Mary, that quickly she arose and went out, followed her, supposing that she was withdrawing unto the tomb, that she might weep there.

text.NRSV: When Mary came where Jesus was and saw him, she knelt at his feet and said to him, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
text.Rotherham: Mary, therefore, when she came where Jesus was, seeing him, fell at his feet, saying unto him—Lord! if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her also weeping, he was greatly disturbed in spirit and deeply moved.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, when he saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her weeping, was indignant in the spirit, and troubled himself,

text.NRSV: He said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to him, “Lord, come and see.”
text.Rotherham: and said—Where have ye laid him? They say unto him—Lord! come and see.

text.NRSV: Jesus began to weep.
text.Rotherham: Jesus wept.

text.NRSV: So the Jews said, “See how he loved him!”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were saying—See! how tenderly he loved him!

text.NRSV: But some of them said, “Could not he who opened the eyes of the blind man have kept this man from dying?”
text.Rotherham: But, some from among them, said—Could not this man, who opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that, this one also, should not have died?

text.NRSV: Then Jesus, again greatly disturbed, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone was lying against it.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, again, being indignant within himself, cometh unto the tomb. Now it was a cave, and, a stone, was lying thereon.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of the dead man, said to him, “Lord, already there is a stench because he has been dead four days.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, saith, Take ye away the stone! Martha, the sister of the deceased, saith unto him—Lord! by this time, he stinketh, for it is, four days.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Did I not tell you that if you believed, you would see the glory of God?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldst believe, thou shouldst see the glory of God?

text.NRSV: So they took away the stone. And Jesus looked upward and said, “Father, I thank you for having heard me.
text.Rotherham: So they took away the stone. And, Jesus, lifted up his eyes on high, and said—Father! I thank thee, thou didst hear me:

text.Rotherham: I, indeed, knew that, always, unto me, thou dost hearken; but, for the sake of the multitude standing around, I spake,—that they might believe that, thou, didst send me forth.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he cried with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”
text.Rotherham: And, these things, having said, with a loud voice, he cried out—Lazarus! Come forth!

text.Rotherham: He that was dead came forth, bound feet and hands with bandages, and, his face, with a napkin, was bound about. Jesus saith unto them—Loose him, and let him go.

text.NRSV: Many of the Jews therefore, who had come with Mary and had seen what Jesus did, believed in him.
text.Rotherham: Many therefore from among the Jews, who had come unto Mary, and gazed on what he did, believed on him

text.Rotherham: but, certain from among them, went away unto the Pharisees, and told them what Jesus had done.

text.Rotherham: The High-priests and Pharisees, therefore, brought together a high-council, and were saying—What are we to do, in that, this man, doeth, many signs?

text.Rotherham: If we let him alone thus, all, will believe on him, and the Romans will come, and take away, both our place and nation.

text.NRSV: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all!
text.Rotherham: But, a certain one from among them, Caiaphas, being, High-priest, for that year, said unto them—Ye, know, nothing at all;

text.Rotherham: nor do ye take into account, that it is profitable for you that, one man, should die for the people, and not, the whole nation, perish.

text.NRSV: He did not say this on his own, but being high priest that year he prophesied that Jesus was about to die for the nation,
text.Rotherham: This, however, from himself, he spake not, but, being, High-priest, for that year, he prophesied, that Jesus was about to die for the nation;

text.NRSV: and not for the nation only, but to gather into one the dispersed children of God.
text.Rotherham: and, not for the nation only, but that, the scattered children of God also, he might gather together into one.

text.NRSV: So from that day on they planned to put him to death.
text.Rotherham: From that day, therefore, they took counsel, that they might put him to death.

text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, no longer openly, was walking among the Jews, but departed thence, into the country near the desert, unto a city called, Ephraim; and, there, abode, with his disciples.

text.NRSV: Now the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover to purify themselves.
text.Rotherham: Now the passover of the Jews was at hand, and many went up unto Jerusalem, out of the country, before the passover, that they might purify themselves.

text.NRSV: They were looking for Jesus and were asking one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think? Surely he will not come to the festival, will he?”
text.Rotherham: They were therefore seeking Jesus, and were saying one to another, in the temple, standing—How seemeth it unto you? that he will in nowise come unto the feast?

text.NRSV: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should let them know, so that they might arrest him.
text.Rotherham: Now the High-priests and the Pharisees had given commands, that, if anyone came to know where he was, he should inform [them], so that they might seize him.

text.NRSV: Six days before the Passover Jesus came to Bethany, the home of Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, six days before the passover, came unto Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from among the dead.

text.NRSV: There they gave a dinner for him. Martha served, and Lazarus was one of those at the table with him.
text.Rotherham: So they made for him a supper, there; and, Martha, was ministering, but, Lazarus, was one of them who were reclining with him.

text.NRSV: Mary took a pound of costly perfume made of pure nard, anointed Jesus’ feet, and wiped them with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
text.Rotherham: Mary, therefore, taking a pound of pure nard perfume, very precious, anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped, with her hair, his feet; and, the house, was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.

text.NRSV: But Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples (the one who was about to betray him), said,
text.Rotherham: [But] Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples, he that was about to deliver him up, saith—

text.Rotherham: Why was, this perfume, not sold for three hundred denaries, and given unto the destitute?

text.NRSV: (He said this not because he cared about the poor, but because he was a thief; he kept the common purse and used to steal what was put into it.)
text.Rotherham: Howbeit he said this, not that, for the destitute, he cared, but because, a thief, he was, and holding, the bag, used to carry away, what was cast therein.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She bought it so that she might keep it for the day of my burial.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Let her alone, that, for the day of my burial, she may observe it;

text.NRSV: You always have the poor with you, but you do not always have me.”
text.Rotherham: For, the destitute, always, have ye with you, whereas, me, not always, have ye.

text.NRSV: When the great crowd of the Jews learned that he was there, they came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
text.Rotherham: The great multitude of the Jews, therefore, got to know that he was, there, and came, not on account of Jesus only, but that, Lazarus also, they might see, whom he had raised from among the dead.

text.NRSV: So the chief priests planned to put Lazarus to death as well,
text.Rotherham: But the High-priests took counsel, that Lazarus also, they might put to death;

text.NRSV: since it was on out of him that many of the Jews were deserting and were believing in Jesus.
text.Rotherham: because, many of the Jews, by reason of him, were withdrawing, and believing on Jesus.

text.Rotherham: took the branches of the palm trees, and went out to meet him, and began crying aloud—Hosanna! Blessed is he that is coming in the name of the Lord,—even the King of Israel!

text.Rotherham: Do not fear, O daughter of Zion! Lo! thy king, cometh, sitting upon the colt of an ass.

text.Rotherham: The multitude, therefore, that was with him when he called, Lazarus, out of the tomb, and raised him from among the dead, was bearing witness.

text.NRSV: It was also because they heard that he had performed this sign that the crowd went to meet him.
text.Rotherham: On this account, the multitude met him also, because they heard that he had done, this sign.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees then said to one another, “You see, you can do nothing. Look, the world has gone after him!”
text.Rotherham: the Pharisees, therefore, said among themselves—Ye observe that ye are profiting nothing: See! the world, after him, hath gone away.

text.Rotherham: Now there were certain Greeks, from among them who were coming up that they might worship in the feast.

text.NRSV: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and said to him, “Sir, we wish to see Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: These, therefore, came unto Philip, him who was from Bethsaida of Galilee,—and were requesting him, saying—Sir! We desire to see, Jesus.

text.NRSV: Philip went and told Andrew; then Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Philip cometh, and telleth Andrew: Andrew and Philip come, and tell Jesus.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, answereth them, saying—The hour hath come, that the Son of Man should be glorified!

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains just a single grain; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.
text.Rotherham: Verily! Verily, I say unto you: Except, the kernel of wheat, shall fall into the ground, and die, it, alone, abideth; but, if it die, much fruit, it beareth.

text.Rotherham: He that loveth his life, loseth it; but, he that hateth his life, in this world, unto life age-abiding, shall guard it.

text.Rotherham: If, with me, anyone be ministering, with me, let him be following; and, where, I, am, there, my minister also, shall be. If anyone, with me, be ministering, the Father, will honour him.

text.Rotherham: There came, therefore, a voice out of heaven—I both have glorified it, and will glorify it again.

text.NRSV: Now is the judgment of this world; now the ruler of this world will be driven out.

text.Rotherham: And, I, if I be lifted up out of the earth, will draw, all, unto myself.

text.Rotherham: The multitude, therefore, answered him—We, have heard, out of the law, that, the Christ, abideth evermore; How then dost, thou, say,—It behoveth the Son of Man to be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?

text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto them—Yet a little while, the light, is, among you: Walk, while ye have, the light, lest, darkness, on you should lay hold; and, he that walketh in darkness, knoweth not whither he drifteth.

text.Rotherham: While, the light, ye have, believe on the light, that, sons of light, ye may become. These things, spake Jesus,—and, departing, was hid from them.

text.NRSV: Although he had performed so many signs in their presence, they did not believe in him.

text.Rotherham: that, the word of Isaiah the prophet, might be fulfilled, which said—Lord! who believed, what we have heard? And, the arm of the Lord, to whom, was it revealed?

text.NRSV: And so they could not believe, because Isaiah also said,

text.Rotherham: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; lest they should see with their eyes, and should understand with their heart, and should turn,—and I should heal them.

text.NRSV: Isaiah said this because he saw his glory and spoke about him.
text.Rotherham: These things, said Isaiah, because he saw his glory, and spake concerning him.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless many, even of the authorities, believed in him. But because of the Pharisees they did not confess it, for fear that they would be put out of the synagogue;
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, however, even from among the rulers, many believed on him; but, because of the Pharisees, they were not confessing him, lest, excommunicants from the synagogue, they should be made;

text.NRSV: for they loved human glory more than the glory that comes from God.
text.Rotherham: for they loved the glory of men, more than the glory of God.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus cried aloud: “Whoever believes in me believes not in me but in him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, cried aloud, and said—He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me;

text.NRSV: And whoever sees me sees him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, he that vieweth me, vieweth him that sent me.

text.NRSV: I have come as light into the world, so that everyone who believes in me should not remain in the darkness.
text.Rotherham: I, a light, into the world have come, that, no one who believeth on me, in darkness, should abide.

text.Rotherham: And, if anyone shall hearken unto my sayings, and not guard them, I, am not judging him; for I came not that I might judge the world, but that I might save the world.

text.NRSV: The one who rejects me and does not receive my word has a judge; on the last day the word that I have spoken will serve as judge,
text.Rotherham: He that setteth me aside, and receiveth not my sayings, hath that which is to judge him: The word that I spake, that, will judge him, in the last day.

text.NRSV: for I have not spoken on my own, but the Father who sent me has himself given me a commandment about what to say and what to speak.
text.Rotherham: Because, I, out of myself, spake not, but, the Father who sent me, hath, himself, given me commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.

text.Rotherham: And I know that, his commandment, is, life age-abiding; The things, therefore, which I speak, just as the Father hath told me, so, I speak.

text.NRSV: Now before the festival of the Passover, Jesus knew that his hour had come to depart from this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.
text.Rotherham: Now, before the feast of the passover, Jesus, knowing that his hour had come, that he should remove out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own that were in the world, unto the end, loved them.

text.NRSV: The devil had already put it into the heart of Judas son of Simon Iscariot to betray him. And during supper

text.NRSV: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he had come from God and was going to God,
text.Rotherham: [Jesus] knowing that, all things, the Father had given unto him, into his hands, and that, from God, he had come, and, unto God, he was going,

text.NRSV: got up from the table, took off his outer robe, and tied a towel around his waist.
text.Rotherham: rouseth himself out of the supper, and layeth aside his garments, and taking a linen cloth, girded himself.

text.Rotherham: Next, he poureth water into the wash-basin, and began to be washing the feet of the disciples, and to be wiping them with the linen cloth wherewith he was girded.

text.NRSV: He came to Simon Peter, who said to him, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
text.Rotherham: So he cometh unto Simon Peter. He saith unto him—Lord, dost, thou, wash my feet?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “You do not know now what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—What, I, am doing, thou, knowest not, as yet; howbeit, thou shalt got to know, hereafter.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “You will never wash my feet.” Jesus answered, “Unless I wash you, you have no share with me.”
text.Rotherham: Peter saith unto him—In nowise shalt thou, ever, wash my feet. Jesus answered him—If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, not my feet only but also my hands and my head!”
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter saith unto him—Lord! not my feet only, but my hands also, and my head.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “One who has bathed does not need to wash, except for the feet, but is entirely clean. And you are clean, though not all of you.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—He that hath bathed himself, hath no need [save as to the feet] to get washed; but is pure, as a whole. And, ye, are, pure, but not ye, all.

text.NRSV: For he knew who was to betray him; for this reason he said, “Not all of you are clean.”
text.Rotherham: For he know the man that was delivering him up; therefore, said he—Not ye all, are pure.

text.NRSV: After he had washed their feet, had put on his robe, and had returned to the table, he said to them, “Do you know what I have done to you?
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had washed their feet, and taken his garments, and reclined, again said he unto them—Are ye taking note, what I have done unto you?

text.NRSV: You call me Teacher and Lord—and you are right, for that is what I am.
text.Rotherham: Ye, call me, The Teacher, and, The Lord,—and, well, say, for I am.

text.NRSV: So if I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another’s feet.
text.Rotherham: If then, I, have washed your feet,—[I] The Lord, and, The Teacher, ye also, ought to wash, one another’s, feet;

text.NRSV: For I have set you an example, that you also should do as I have done to you.
text.Rotherham: For, an example, have I given you,—that, just as, I, did unto you, ye also, should be doing.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, servants are not greater than their master, nor are messengers greater than the one who sent them.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—A servant is not greater than his lord, neither one sent forth greater than he that sent him.

text.NRSV: If you know these things, you are blessed if you do them.
text.Rotherham: If, these things, ye know, happy, are ye, if ye be doing them.

text.NRSV: I am not speaking of all of you; I know whom I have chosen. But it is to fulfill the scripture, ‘The one who ate my bread has lifted his heel against me.’
text.Rotherham: Not, concerning you all, am I speaking,—for, I, know of whom I made choice; but…that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled: He that feedeth on my bread, hath lifted up against me, his heel.

text.NRSV: I tell you this now, before it occurs, so that when it does occur, you may believe that I am he.
text.Rotherham: Henceforth, I tell you, before it cometh to pass,—that ye may believe, whensoever it doth come to pass, that, I, am he.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever receives one whom I send receives me; and whoever receives me receives him who sent me.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—He that receiveth whomsoever I shall send, receiveth, me; and, he that receiveth, me, receiveth him that sent me.

text.NRSV: After saying this Jesus was troubled in spirit, and declared, “Very truly, I tell you, one of you will betray me.”
text.Rotherham: These things, having said, Jesus, was troubled in spirit, and bare witness, and said—Verily, verily, I say unto you—One from among you, will deliver me up.

text.NRSV: The disciples looked at one another, uncertain of whom he was speaking.
text.Rotherham: The disciples began to look one at another, being at a loss concerning whom he was speaking.

text.NRSV: One of his disciples—the one whom Jesus loved—was reclining next to him;
text.Rotherham: One of Jesus’ disciples was reclining in his bosom, one whom Jesus loved:

text.NRSV: Simon Peter therefore motioned to him to ask Jesus of whom he was speaking.
text.Rotherham: so Simon Peter beckoneth unto the same, and saith unto him—Say, Who is it? concerning whom he speaketh.

text.NRSV: So while reclining next to Jesus, he asked him, “Lord, who is it?”
text.Rotherham: He, falling back thus, upon the breast of Jesus, saith unto him—Lord! who is it?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “It is the one to whom I give this piece of bread when I have dipped it in the dish.” So when he had dipped the piece of bread, he gave it to Judas son of Simon Iscariot.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, answereth—That one, it is, for whom, I, shall dip the morsel, and give unto him. So, dipping the morsel, he taketh and giveth it unto Judas, son of Simon Iscariot.

text.NRSV: After he received the piece of bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus said to him, “Do quickly what you are going to do.”
text.Rotherham: And, after the morsel, then, entered, Satan, into that man. Jesus, therefore, saith unto him—What thou art doing, do quickly!

text.NRSV: Now no one at the table knew why he said this to him.
text.Rotherham: But, as to this, none of them who were reclining with him knew respecting what, he said it to him.

text.NRSV: Some thought that, because Judas had the common purse, Jesus was telling him, “Buy what we need for the festival”; or that he should give something to the poor.
text.Rotherham: For, some, were thinking, since Judas held the bag, that Jesus was saying to him—Buy the things of which we have, need, for the feast; or that, unto the destitute, he should give something.

text.NRSV: So, after receiving the piece of bread, he immediately went out. And it was night.
text.Rotherham: So, he, taking the morsel, went out straightway;—and it was night.

text.NRSV: When he had gone out, Jesus said, “Now the Son of Man has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had gone out, Jesus saith—Just now, was, the Son of Man, glorified, and, God, was glorified in him;

text.NRSV: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself and will glorify him at once.
text.Rotherham: And, God, will glorify him, in himself,—and, straightway, glorify him.

text.NRSV: Little children, I am with you only a little longer. You will look for me; and as I said to the Jews so now I say to you, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come.’
text.Rotherham: Dear children! Yet a little, am I, with you. Ye shall seek me, and, just as I said unto the Jews, Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come, unto you also, I say it, even now.

text.NRSV: I give you a new commandment, that you love one another. Just as I have loved you, you also should love one another.
text.Rotherham: A new commandment, give I unto you,—That ye be loving one another: Just as I loved you, that, ye also, be loving one another:

text.NRSV: By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.”
text.Rotherham: By this, shall all men take knowledge, that, my disciples, ye are,—If ye have, love, one to another.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “Where I am going, you cannot follow me now; but you will follow afterward.”
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter saith unto him—Lord! whither dost thou withdraw? Jesus answered—Whither I withdraw, thou canst not, now, follow me, but thou shalt follow, hereafter.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Lord, why can I not follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”
text.Rotherham: Peter saith unto him—Lord! why, cannot I follow thee, even now? My life, in thy behalf, will I lay down.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Will you lay down your life for me? Very truly, I tell you, before the cock crows, you will have denied me three times.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answereth—Thy life, in my behalf, wilt thou lay down? Verily, verily, I say unto thee—In nowise shall a cock crow till thou hast denied me, thrice.

text.NRSV: “Do not let your hearts be troubled. Believe in God, believe also in me.
text.Rotherham: Let not your heart be troubled: Believe on God, and, on me, believe.

text.NRSV: In my Father’s house there are many dwelling places. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you?
text.Rotherham: In the house of my Father, are, many dwellings; or else I would have told you, I go to prepare a place for you.

text.NRSV: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will take you to myself, so that where I am, there you may be also.
text.Rotherham: And, if I go, and prepare a place for you, again, am I coming, and will take you home unto—myself, that, where, I, am, ye also, may be.

text.NRSV: And you know the way to the place where I am going.”
text.Rotherham: And, whither, I, go, ye know, the way.

text.NRSV: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we do not know where you are going. How can we know the way?”
text.Rotherham: Thomas saith unto him—Lord! we know not whither thou goest: How know we, the way?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—I, am the way, and the truth, and the life: No one, cometh unto the Father, but through me.

text.NRSV: If you know me, you will know my Father also. From now on you do know him and have seen him.”
text.Rotherham: If ye had been getting to know me, my Father also, had ye known: from henceforth, are ye getting to know him, and have seen him.

text.NRSV: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and we will be satisfied.”
text.Rotherham: Philip saith unto him—Lord! show us the Father, and it sufficeth us.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you all this time, Philip, and you still do not know me? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him—So long a time as this, have I been, with you,—and thou hast not come to know me, Philip? He that hath seen me, hath seen the Father. How art, thou, saying, Show us the Father?

text.NRSV: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on my own; but the Father who dwells in me does his works.
text.Rotherham: Believest thou not, that, I, am in the Father, and, the Father, is, in me? The things which I am saying unto you, from myself, I speak not; but, the Father, within me abiding, doeth his works.

text.NRSV: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me; but if you do not, then believe me because of the works themselves.
text.Rotherham: Believe me, That, I, am in the Father, and, the Father, in me;—or else, on account of the works themselves, believe ye.

text.NRSV: “Very truly, I tell you, the one who believes in me will also do the works that I do and, in fact, will do greater works than these, because I am going to the Father.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—He that believeth on me, the works which, I, am doing, he also, shall do; and, greater than these, shall he do, because, I, unto the Father, am going,—

text.NRSV: I will do whatever you ask in my name, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
text.Rotherham: And because, whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, the same, will I do, that, the Father, may be glorified, in the Son:

text.NRSV: If in my name you ask me for anything, I will do it.
text.Rotherham: If anything ye shall ask [me] in my name, the same, will I do.

text.NRSV: “If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
text.Rotherham: If ye be loving me, my commandments, ye will keep;

text.NRSV: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Advocate, to be with you forever.
text.Rotherham: And, I, will request the Father, and, Another Advocate, will he give unto you, that he may be with you age-abidingly,

text.NRSV: This is the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees him nor knows him. You know him, because he abides with you, and he will be in you.
text.Rotherham: The Spirit of truth,—which, the world, cannot receive, because it beholdeth it not, nor getteth to know it. But, ye, are getting to know it; because, with you, it abideth, and, in you, it is.

text.NRSV: “I will not leave you orphaned; I am coming to you.
text.Rotherham: I will not leave you bereft,—I am coming unto you.

text.NRSV: In a little while the world will no longer see me, but you will see me; because I live, you also will live.
text.Rotherham: Yet a little, and, the world, no longer beholdeth me; but, ye, behold me,—Because, I, live, ye also, shall live.

text.NRSV: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall ye get to know, That, I, am in my Father, and, ye, in me, and, I, in you.

text.NRSV: They who have my commandments and keep them are those who love me; and those who love me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.”
text.Rotherham: He that hath my commandments and keepeth them, he, it is that loveth me; and, he that loveth me, shall be loved by my Father, and, I, will love him, and will manifest, myself, unto him.

text.NRSV: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, how is it that you will reveal yourself to us, and not to the world?”
text.Rotherham: Judas, not the Iscariot, saith unto him—Lord! what hath happened, that, unto us, thou art about to manifest thyself, and, not unto the world?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “Those who love me will keep my word, and my Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto him—If any man be loving me, my word, he will keep, and, my Father, will love him,—and, unto him, will we come, and, an abode with him, will we make.

text.NRSV: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words; and the word that you hear is not mine, but is from the Father who sent me.
text.Rotherham: He that loveth me not, doth not keep, my word;—and, the word which ye hear, is not mine, but, the Father’s who sent me.

text.NRSV: “I have said these things to you while I am still with you.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, With you abiding;

text.NRSV: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything, and remind you of all that I have said to you.
text.Rotherham: But, the Advocate, The Holy Spirit, which the Father will send in my name, He, will teach you all things, and will put you in mind, of all things which, I, told you.

text.NRSV: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled, and do not let them be afraid.
text.Rotherham: Peace, I leave with you, My own peace, give, I, unto you,—Not as, the world, giveth, give, I, unto you:—Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.

text.NRSV: You heard me say to you, ‘I am going away, and I am coming to you.’ If you loved me, you would rejoice that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I.
text.Rotherham: Ye heard that, I, said unto you—I go my way, and I come unto you,—Had ye loved me, ye would have rejoiced, that I am going unto the Father, for, the Father, is, greater than I.

text.NRSV: And now I have told you this before it occurs, so that when it does occur, you may believe.
text.Rotherham: But, now, have I told you, before it cometh to pass, that, whensoever it shall come to pass, ye may believe.

text.NRSV: I will no longer talk much with you, for the ruler of this world is coming. He has no power over me;
text.Rotherham: No longer, many things, will I speak with you; for, the world’s ruler, is coming, and, in me, hath, nothing,—

text.NRSV: but I do as the Father has commanded me, so that the world may know that I love the Father. Rise, let us be on our way.”
text.Rotherham: But, that the world may get to know that I love the Father, and just as the Father hath given me commandment, so, I do. Be rousing yourselves! let us be leading on from hence.

text.NRSV: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinegrower.
text.Rotherham: I, am the real vine, and, my Father, is, the husbandman.

text.NRSV: He removes every branch in me that bears no fruit. Every branch that bears fruit he prunes to make it bear more fruit.
text.Rotherham: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit, He taketh it away; and, every one that beareth, fruit, He pruneth it, that, more fruit, it may bear.

text.NRSV: You have already been cleansed by the word that I have spoken to you.
text.Rotherham: Already, ye, are, pure, because of the word which I have spoken unto you:

text.NRSV: Abide in me as I abide in you. Just as the branch cannot bear fruit by itself unless it abides in the vine, so neither can you unless you abide in me.
text.Rotherham: Abide in me, and, I, in you. Just as, the branch, cannot be bearing fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine, so, neither, ye, except, in me, ye abide.

text.NRSV: I am the vine, you are the branches. Those who abide in me and I in them bear much fruit, because apart from me you can do nothing.
text.Rotherham: I, am the vine: Ye, are the branches. He that abideth in me and, I, in him, the same, beareth much fruit; because, apart from me, ye can bring forth, nothing.

text.NRSV: Whoever does not abide in me is thrown away like a branch and withers; such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
text.Rotherham: If one abide not me, he is cast out as the branch, and withered, and they gather them,—and, into fire, they cast them, and they are burned.

text.NRSV: If you abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask for whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
text.Rotherham: If ye abide in me, and, my sayings, in you, abide, whatsoever ye may be desiring, ask! and it shall be brought to pass for you.

text.NRSV: My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit and become my disciples.
text.Rotherham: Herein, was my Father glorified, that, much fruit, ye should bear, and become my disciples.

text.NRSV: As the Father has loved me, so I have loved you; abide in my love.
text.Rotherham: Just as the Father loved me, I also, loved you: Abide ye in my love.

text.NRSV: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commandments and abide in his love.
text.Rotherham: If, my commandments, ye keep, ye shall abide in my love,—just as, I, the Father’s commandments, have kept, and abide in his love.

text.NRSV: I have said these things to you so that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be complete.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that, my own joy, in you, may be, and, your joy, may be made full.

text.NRSV: “This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
text.Rotherham: This, is my own commandment, That ye be loving one another, just as I loved you.

text.NRSV: No one has greater love than this, to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
text.Rotherham: Greater love than this, hath, no one, That, his life, one should lay down in behalf of his friends.

text.NRSV: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are, friends of mine, if ye be doing that which, I, am commanding you.

text.NRSV: I do not call you servants any longer, because the servant does not know what the master is doing; but I have called you friends, because I have made known to you everything that I have heard from my Father.
text.Rotherham: No longer, do I call you, servants, for, the servant, knoweth not what, his lord, is doing. But, you, I have called, friends, because, all things which I heard from my Father, made I known unto you.

text.NRSV: You did not choose me but I chose you. And I appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, so that the Father will give you whatever you ask him in my name.
text.Rotherham: Not, ye, chose, me, but, I, chose you, and placed you, that ye should go your way and bear, fruit,—and, your fruit, should abide: that, whatsoever ye should ask the Father in my name, he might give unto you.

text.NRSV: I am giving you these commands so that you may love one another.
text.Rotherham: These things, I command you, That ye be loving one another.

text.NRSV: “If the world hates you, be aware that it hated me before it hated you.
text.Rotherham: If, the world, is hating, you, ye are getting to know that, me, before you, it hath hated.

text.NRSV: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. Because you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world—therefore the world hates you.
text.Rotherham: If, of the world, ye had been, the world, of its own, had been fond; but, because, of the world, ye are not, on the contrary, I, chose you out of the world, therefore, the world, doth hate you.

text.NRSV: Remember the word that I said to you, ‘Servants are not greater than their master.’ If they persecuted me, they will persecute you; if they kept my word, they will keep yours also.
text.Rotherham: Remember the word which, I, spake unto you: A servant is not greater than his lord. If, me, they persecuted, you too, will they persecute,—If, my word, they kept, your own also, will they keep.

text.NRSV: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they do not know the one who sent me.
text.Rotherham: But, all these things, will they do unto you, on account of my name, because they know not him that sent me.

text.NRSV: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
text.Rotherham: Had I not come and spoken unto them, Sin, had they none; but, now, have they no, excuse, for their sin.

text.NRSV: Whoever hates me hates my Father also.
text.Rotherham: He that hateth me, hateth, my Father also.

text.NRSV: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not have sin; but now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
text.Rotherham: Had I not done among them, the works, which, no other, had done, sin, had they none; but, now, have they, both seen and hated both me and my Father.

text.NRSV: It was to fulfill the word that is written in their law, ‘They hated me without a cause.’
text.Rotherham: But…that the word which, in their law, is written, might be fulfilled—They hated me without cause.

text.NRSV: “When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who comes from the Father, he will testify on my behalf.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever the Advocate shall come, Whom, I, will send unto you from the Father, The Spirit of truth, which, from the Father, cometh forth, He, will bear witness concerning me;

text.NRSV: You also are to testify because you have been with me from the beginning.”
text.Rotherham: And do, ye also, bear witness, because, from the beginning, ye are, with me,

text.NRSV: “I have said these things to you to keep you from stumbling.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that ye may not be caused to stumble:

text.NRSV: They will put you out of the synagogues. Indeed, an hour is coming when those who kill you will think that by doing so they are offering worship to God.
text.Rotherham: Excommunicants from the synagogue, will they make you; Nay! there cometh an hour, that, every one who killeth you, shall think to be rendering, divine service, unto God!

text.NRSV: And they will do this because they have not known the Father or me.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, will they do, because they got to know, neither the Father nor me.

text.NRSV: But I have said these things to you so that when their hour comes you may remember that I told you of them. “I did not say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
text.Rotherham: But, these things, have I told you,—That, whensoever their hour shall come, ye may remember, that, thereof, I told you. These things, however, I told you not, from the beginning, because I was, with you;

text.NRSV: But now I am going to him who sent me; yet none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
text.Rotherham: But, now, I go my way unto him that sent me, and, not one from among you, questioneth me—Whither goest thou?

text.NRSV: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your hearts.
text.Rotherham: But, because, these things, I have told you, sorrow, hath filled your heart.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
text.Rotherham: But, I, the truth, am telling you—It is profitable for you, that, I, depart; for, if I should not depart, The Advocate, would in nowise come unto you, but, if I go, I will send him unto you.

text.NRSV: And when he comes, he will prove the world wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment:
text.Rotherham: And, having come, He, will reprove the world—Concerning sin, and concerning righteousness, and concerning judgment:

text.NRSV: about sin, because they do not believe in me;
text.Rotherham: Concerning sin, indeed, because they are not believing on me;

text.NRSV: about righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will see me no longer;
text.Rotherham: But, concerning righteousness, because, unto the Father, I go my way, and, no longer, do ye behold me;

text.NRSV: about judgment, because the ruler of this world has been condemned.
text.Rotherham: And, concerning judgment, because, the ruler of this world, hath been judged.
